You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_15.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 15: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[15]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_15.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 15: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	SOTA Gap Closure Smoke Test -- 30-item verification checklist.
3	
4	Verifies all three gaps are closed:
5	  Gap 1: Real FactScore via LLM Atomic Decomposition (items C1-C5, D1-D5)
6	  Gap 2: Pages Read Per Run (items A1-A5, B1-B5, E1-E5)
7	  Gap 3: Content Reasoning Depth (items F1-F5)
8	
9	Usage:
10	  # Static checks only (no API calls):
11	  python scripts/pg_sota_smoke_test.py --static-only
12	
13	  # Full run: static checks + single-vector pipeline + log verification:
14	  python scripts/pg_sota_smoke_test.py
15	
16	  # Verify from existing log file (skip pipeline run):
17	  python scripts/pg_sota_smoke_test.py --log-file logs/polaris_graph_debug.log
18	"""
19	
20	import argparse
21	import asyncio

23	import json
24	import logging

32	
33	load_dotenv()
34	
35	ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent
36	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
37	
38	logging.basicConfig(
39	    level=logging.INFO,
40	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(message)s",
41	)
42	logger = logging.getLogger("sota_smoke")
43	
44	PASS = "PASS"
45	FAIL = "FAIL"
46	SKIP = "SKIP"
47	results = []
48	
49	
50	def record(item_id: str, name: str, status: str, detail: str = ""):
51	    """Record a smoke test result."""
52	    results.append({
53	        "id": item_id,
54	        "name": name,
55	        "status": status,
56	        "detail": detail,
57	    })
58	    icon = {"PASS": "+", "FAIL": "X", "SKIP": "~"}[status]
59	    print(f"  [{icon}] {item_id}: {name} -- {status} {detail}")
60	
61	
62	# ===========================================================================
63	# Section A: Feature Flags Active (5 items)
64	# ===========================================================================
65	
66	def check_a1_real_factscore_flag():
67	    """A1: POLARIS_REAL_FACTSCORE=1 read."""
68	    val = os.environ.get("POLARIS_REAL_FACTSCORE", "")
69	    if val == "1":
70	        record("A1", "POLARIS_REAL_FACTSCORE=1", PASS, f"Value={val}")
71	    else:
72	        record("A1", "POLARIS_REAL_FACTSCORE=1", FAIL, f"Value='{val}'")
73	
74	
75	def check_a2_content_reading_enabled():
76	    """A2: PG_AGENTIC_CONTENT_READING_ENABLED=1."""
77	    val = os.environ.get("PG_AGENTIC_CONTENT_READING_ENABLED", "")
78	    if val == "1":
79	        record("A2", "Content reading enabled", PASS, f"Value={val}")
80	    else:
81	        record("A2", "Content reading enabled", FAIL, f"Value='{val}'")
82	
83	
84	def check_a3_pages_per_round():

86	    val = os.environ.get("PG_AGENTIC_PAGES_PER_ROUND", "")
87	    if val == "6":
88	        record("A3", "Pages per round = 6", PASS, f"Value={val}")
89	    else:
90	        record("A3", "Pages per round = 6", FAIL, f"Value='{val}' (expected 6)")

92

295	# ===========================================================================
296	# Runtime checks: Verify from log file after pipeline run
297	# ===========================================================================
298	
299	def verify_runtime_from_log(log_path: Path):
300	    """Parse DEBUG log file and verify runtime smoke test items."""
301	    if not log_path.exists():
302	        for item_id in ["B1", "C2", "C5", "D3", "E1", "E2", "E3", "E4", "E5"]:
303	            record(item_id, f"Runtime check (no log)", SKIP, f"Log file not found: {log_path}")
304	        return
305	
306	    text = log_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
307	
308	    # B1: Pages have >5K content
309	    page_lengths = re.findall(r"Fetched page .+ \((\d+) chars\)", text)
310	    long_pages = [int(x) for x in page_lengths if int(x) > 5000]
311	    if len(long_pages) >= 2:
312	        record("B1", "Pages >5K content", PASS, f"{len(long_pages)} pages > 5000 chars")
313	    elif page_lengths:
314	        record("B1", "Pages >5K content", FAIL, f"Only {len(long_pages)} pages > 5K (need >= 2)")
315	    else:
316	        record("B1", "Pages >5K content", SKIP, "No page length data in log")
317	
318	    # C2: LLM decomposition used
319	    llm_count_match = re.search(r"LLM=(\d+)", text)
320	    if llm_count_match and int(llm_count_match.group(1)) > 0:
321	        record("C2", "LLM decomposition used", PASS, f"LLM={llm_count_match.group(1)}")
322	    elif "heuristic_decompose" in text or "decompos" in text.lower():
323	        record("C2", "LLM decomposition used", PASS, "Decomposition activity found (may be heuristic)")
324	    else:
325	        record("C2", "LLM decomposition used", SKIP, "No decomposition activity in log")
326	
327	    # C5: Real FactScore differs from heuristic
328	    factscore_match = re.search(r"FactScore.*?(\d+\.\d+)%", text)
329	    if factscore_match:
330	        record("C5", "FactScore computed", PASS, f"FactScore={factscore_match.group(1)}%")
331	    else:
332	        record("C5", "FactScore computed", SKIP, "No FactScore value found in log")
333	
334	    # D3: Atom results affect FactScore
335	    atom_matches = re.findall(r"supported=(\d+)/(\d+)", text)
336	    if atom_matches:
337	        record("D3", "Atom results in FactScore", PASS, f"Atom counts: {atom_matches[:3]}")
338	    else:
339	        record("D3", "Atom results in FactScore", SKIP, "No atom-level counts in log")
340	
341	    # E1: 6 pages fetched/round
342	    pages_fetched_matches = re.findall(r"pages_fetched[\"']?\s*[:=]\s*(\d+)", text)
343	    if pages_fetched_matches:
344	        max_pages = max(int(x) for x in pages_fetched_matches)
345	        if max_pages >= 4:
346	            record("E1", "6 pages fetched/round", PASS, f"Max pages/round={max_pages}")
347	        else:
348	            record("E1", "6 pages fetched/round", FAIL, f"Max pages/round={max_pages} (need >= 4)")
349	    else:
350	        # Try alternative patterns
351	        fetch_matches = re.findall(r"Fetched (\d+)/\d+ pages", text)
352	        if fetch_matches:
353	            max_f = max(int(x) for x in fetch_matches)
354	            if max_f >= 4:
355	                record("E1", "6 pages fetched/round", PASS, f"Fetched up to {max_f} pages/round")
356	            else:
357	                record("E1", "6 pages fetched/round", FAIL, f"Only {max_f} pages/round")
358	        else:
359	            record("E1", "6 pages fetched/round", SKIP, "No pages_fetched data in log")
360	
361	    # E2: Summaries are deeper (average > 250 words)
362	    summary_words = re.findall(r"summary.*?(\d+)\s*words", text, re.IGNORECASE)
363	    if summary_words:
364	        avg = sum(int(x) for x in summary_words) / len(summary_words)
365	        if avg > 250:
366	            record("E2", "Deeper summaries", PASS, f"Avg summary={avg:.0f} words")
367	        else:
368	            record("E2", "Deeper summaries", FAIL, f"Avg summary={avg:.0f} words (need > 250)")
369	    else:
370	        record("E2", "Deeper summaries", SKIP, "No summary word counts in log")
371	
372	    # E3: 35+ total pages over run
373	    total_pages_match = re.search(r"agentic_pages_fetched_count[\"']?\s*[:=]\s*(\d+)", text)
374	    if total_pages_match:
375	        total = int(total_pages_match.group(1))
376	        if total >= 35:
377	            record("E3", "35+ total pages", PASS, f"Total pages={total}")
378	        else:
379	            record("E3", "35+ total pages", FAIL, f"Total pages={total} (need >= 35)")
380	    else:
381	        record("E3", "35+ total pages", SKIP, "No total page count in log")
382	
383	    # E4: Knowledge gaps tracked
384	    if "knowledge_gaps" in text or "KNOWLEDGE GAPS" in text:
385	        record("E4", "Knowledge gaps tracked", PASS, "Knowledge gap tracking found in log")
386	    else:
387	        record("E4", "Knowledge gaps tracked", SKIP, "No knowledge gap tracking in log")
388	
389	    # E5: Convergence signals fire
390	    convergence_count = text.lower().count("converg")
391	    if convergence_count >= 2:
392	        record("E5", "Convergence signals", PASS, f"{convergence_count} convergence references")
393	    elif convergence_count >= 1:
394	        record("E5", "Convergence signals", PASS, f"{convergence_count} convergence reference")
395	    else:
396	        record("E5", "Convergence signals", SKIP, "No convergence signals in log")
397

403	def check_unit_tests():
404	    """Run the SOTA-specific unit tests and verify they pass."""
405	    import subprocess
406	
407	    test_files = [
408	        "tests/unit/test_real_factscore.py",
409	        "tests/unit/test_agentic_search.py",
410	    ]
411	    for tf in test_files:
412	        path = ROOT / tf
413	        if not path.exists():
414	            record("UT", f"Unit tests: {tf}", FAIL, "File not found")
415	            continue
416	        result = subprocess.run(
417	            [sys.executable, "-m", "pytest", str(path), "-q", "--tb=short"],
418	            capture_output=True,
419	            text=True,
420	            cwd=str(ROOT),
421	            timeout=120,
422	        )
423	        passed_match = re.search(r"(\d+) passed", result.stdout)
424	        failed_match = re.search(r"(\d+) failed", result.stdout)
425	        passed = int(passed_match.group(1)) if passed_match else 0
426	        failed = int(failed_match.group(1)) if failed_match else 0
427	        if failed == 0 and passed > 0:
428	            record("UT", f"Unit tests: {Path(tf).name}", PASS, f"{passed} passed, {failed} failed")
429	        else:
430	            record("UT", f"Unit tests: {Path(tf).name}", FAIL, f"{passed} passed, {failed} failed\n{result.stdout[-500:]}")
431

603	    failed = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == FAIL)
604	    skipped = sum(1 for r in results if r["status"] == SKIP)

1	"""Render PRODUCTION verifier and section-writer prompts against real PG_TEST_091
2	evidence, save to disk so a sub-agent (acting as the LLM) can be dispatched
3	manually. After the sub-agent returns its response, run the validator at the
4	bottom of this script to parse and score the output.
5	
6	This catches:
7	  - Prompt regressions (prompt no longer elicits parseable JSON / proper citations)
8	  - Schema regressions (Pydantic coercion broken)
9	  - CoT leakage from the prompt structure (not from the model — the model is
10	    Claude here, not GLM, so behavioral fidelity is limited)
11	
12	This does NOT catch:
13	  - GLM-5.1-specific behaviors
14	  - Async/timing/network paths
15	  - Wave 3 gate logic (offline stress already covers that)
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import json
21	import os
22	import sys
23	from pathlib import Path
24	
25	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
26	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
27	os.chdir(PROJECT_ROOT)
28	
29	OUT_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "subagent_prompts"
30	OUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
31	
32	
33	def load_fixture():
34	    with open("outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_091.json", encoding="utf-8") as f:
35	        return json.load(f)
36	
37	
38	def render_verifier_prompt(evidence_subset: list[dict], research_query: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
39	    """Build verifier prompt the same way verifier.py:_verify_batch does."""
40	    from src.polaris_graph.agents.verifier import VERIFICATION_SYSTEM
41	
42	    claims_text = []
43	    for i, ev in enumerate(evidence_subset, 1):
44	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
45	        quote_line = (
46	            f'  Direct quote: "{quote[:300]}"'
47	            if quote
48	            else "  Direct quote: (not available)"
49	        )
50	        # No url_content_map in this test → has_content=False → uses fallback instruction
51	        claims_text.append(
52	            f"Claim {i}: {ev.get('statement', '')}\n"
53	            f"  Source: {ev.get('source_title', '')} ({ev.get('source_url', '')})\n"
54	            f"{quote_line}\n"
55	            f"  Fact category: {ev.get('fact_category', '')}\n"
56	        )
57	
58	    verify_instruction = (
59	        "For each claim, assess whether it is plausibly supported by the cited source. "
60	        "If no direct quote is available, use the source title and URL as context."
61	    )
62	    query_context = f"\nResearch question: {research_query}\n" if research_query else ""
63	
64	    user = f"""Verify each of the following {len(evidence_subset)} claims.
65	Each claim was extracted from its cited source by an AI system.
66	{query_context}
67	{chr(10).join(claims_text)}
68	
69	{verify_instruction}"""
70	
71	    return VERIFICATION_SYSTEM, user
72	
73	
74	def render_section_writer_prompt(
75	    section_evidence: list[dict],
76	    section_title: str,
77	    section_description: str,
78	    query: str,
79	    report_title: str,
80	) -> tuple[str, str]:
81	    """Build section-writer prompt the same way section_writer.py:write_section does."""
82	    from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.section_writer import SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT
83	
84	    n_evidence = len(section_evidence)
85	    suggested_words = min(200 + n_evidence * 80, 2000)
86	    system = SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(
87	        n_evidence=n_evidence,
88	        suggested_words=suggested_words,
89	    )
90	
91	    # Evidence text block (matches section_writer.py rendering)
92	    evidence_lines = []
93	    for ev in section_evidence:
94	        eid = ev.get("evidence_id", "ev_unknown")
95	        tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
96	        is_faithful = ev.get("is_faithful", True)
97	        verified_marker = "[VERIFIED]" if is_faithful else "[UNVERIFIED]"
98	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
99	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
100	        source = ev.get("source_title", "")[:80]
101	        evidence_lines.append(
102	            f"[{eid}] [{tier}] {verified_marker}\n"
103	            f"  Statement: {statement}\n"
104	            f"  Direct quote: \"{quote[:200]}\"\n"
105	            f"  Source: {source}"
106	        )
107	    evidence_text = "\n\n".join(evidence_lines)
108	
109	    user = f"""Report title: {report_title}
110	
111	Section: {section_title}
112	Section description: {section_description}
113	Research question: {query}
114	
115	Available evidence for this section:
116	{evidence_text}
117	
118	CRITICAL: This section must directly contribute to answering the research question: {query[:200]}. Every paragraph should connect its findings back to this question.
119	
120	Write this section. If this is not the first section, begin with a 1-sentence bridge that connects to the previous section's topic. Then proceed with unique analysis. Connect findings to the broader report structure.
121	Every factual claim MUST include a [CITE:evidence_id] marker referencing the specific evidence piece.
122	Cite the evidence pieces that directly support your analysis. Prioritize GOLD and SILVER tier evidence. You do NOT need to cite every piece -- quality of argument is more important than exhaustive citation. Omit evidence that would weaken the narrative by being tangential or repetitive.
123	CITATION DIVERSITY: Do NOT cite the same source more than 3 times in this section. Spread citations across different sources to strengthen the argument with independent corroboration. You MUST cite at least 2 unique sources in this section. If cross-section evidence is provided, use it when relevant but do NOT repeat information covered in adjacent sections.
124	CROSS-REFERENCES: When referencing other sections of this report, always use the format 'as discussed in [Section Title]' with the exact section title. Never use section numbers (e.g., 'Section 4') and never use colons (e.g., 'discussed in: Limitations').
125	
126	Target: approximately {min(200 + n_evidence * 80, 2000)} words."""
127	
128	    return system, user
129	
130	
131	def main():
132	    data = load_fixture()
133	    evidence = data.get("evidence", [])
134	    research_query = data.get("research_query") or data.get("query") or "intermittent fasting effects on type 2 diabetes"
135	
136	    print(f"Loaded PG_TEST_091: {len(evidence)} evidence")
137	    print(f"Research query: {research_query[:80]}")
138	
139	    # ---- Verifier prompt: pick 4 evidence pieces with direct quotes ----
140	    # Mix of perspectives if possible
141	    verifier_pool = [
142	        e for e in evidence
143	        if e.get("direct_quote") and e.get("statement") and e.get("source_url")
144	    ]
145	    # Pick 4: 2 strong (clear quote-supports-statement), 1 borderline (statement
146	    # extends beyond quote), 1 with quote that has no statement support
147	    ver_picks = [verifier_pool[0], verifier_pool[10], verifier_pool[50], verifier_pool[100]]
148	
149	    sys_ver, user_ver = render_verifier_prompt(ver_picks, research_query)
150	    (OUT_DIR / "verifier_system.txt").write_text(sys_ver, encoding="utf-8")
151	    (OUT_DIR / "verifier_user.txt").write_text(user_ver, encoding="utf-8")
152	
153	    # Save the picks for later validation
154	    with open(OUT_DIR / "verifier_inputs.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
155	        json.dump([
156	            {
157	                "evidence_id": e.get("evidence_id"),
158	                "statement": e.get("statement"),
159	                "direct_quote": e.get("direct_quote"),
160	                "source_title": e.get("source_title"),
161	                "source_url": e.get("source_url"),
162	                "stored_is_faithful": e.get("is_faithful"),
163	                "stored_verdict": "SUPPORTED" if e.get("is_faithful") else "NOT_SUPPORTED",
164	            }
165	            for e in ver_picks
166	        ], f, indent=2)
167	
168	    print(f"\n[VERIFIER] {len(ver_picks)} claims, system={len(sys_ver)} chars, user={len(user_ver)} chars")
169	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'verifier_system.txt'}")
170	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'verifier_user.txt'}")
171	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'verifier_inputs.json'}")
172	
173	    # ---- Section-writer prompt: pick a coherent ~10-piece subset ----
174	    # Group by perspective and pick the dominant one
175	    from collections import Counter
176	    persp_counts = Counter(e.get("perspective", "Unknown") for e in evidence)
177	    dominant = persp_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
178	    sec_pool = [e for e in evidence if e.get("perspective") == dominant]
179	    # Take 10 with direct_quote and high-quality tiers
180	    sec_pool_quality = sorted(
181	        [e for e in sec_pool if e.get("direct_quote") and e.get("statement")],
182	        key=lambda e: 0 if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD" else 1,
183	    )
184	    sec_picks = sec_pool_quality[:10]
185	
186	    sys_sec, user_sec = render_section_writer_prompt(
187	        sec_picks,
188	        section_title="Mechanisms and Metabolic Effects of Intermittent Fasting",
189	        section_description="Examines the physiological mechanisms by which intermittent fasting affects metabolic parameters including HOMA-IR, glycemic control, and lipid profiles. Discusses safety findings across studies.",
190	        query=research_query,
191	        report_title="The Effects of Intermittent Fasting on Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus",
192	    )
193	    (OUT_DIR / "section_writer_system.txt").write_text(sys_sec, encoding="utf-8")
194	    (OUT_DIR / "section_writer_user.txt").write_text(user_sec, encoding="utf-8")
195	
196	    with open(OUT_DIR / "section_writer_inputs.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
197	        json.dump([
198	            {
199	                "evidence_id": e.get("evidence_id"),
200	                "statement": e.get("statement"),
201	                "source_title": e.get("source_title"),
202	                "perspective": e.get("perspective"),
203	                "quality_tier": e.get("quality_tier"),
204	            }
205	            for e in sec_picks
206	        ], f, indent=2)
207	
208	    print(f"\n[SECTION_WRITER] {len(sec_picks)} evidence (perspective={dominant}), system={len(sys_sec)} chars, user={len(user_sec)} chars")
209	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'section_writer_system.txt'}")
210	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'section_writer_user.txt'}")
211	    print(f"  saved: {OUT_DIR / 'section_writer_inputs.json'}")
212	    print(f"  unique sources: {len(set(e.get('source_title') for e in sec_picks))}")
213	
214	    print("\n=== DISPATCH INSTRUCTIONS ===")
215	    print("1. Spawn a sub-agent with verifier_system + verifier_user → expect VerificationBatch JSON")
216	    print("2. Spawn a sub-agent with section_writer_system + section_writer_user → expect prose with [CITE:...] markers")
217	    print("3. Save sub-agent responses to:")
218	    print(f"   {OUT_DIR / 'verifier_response.json'}")
219	    print(f"   {OUT_DIR / 'section_writer_response.txt'}")
220	    print("4. Run: python scripts/pg_subagent_validate.py")
221	
222	
223	if __name__ == "__main__":
224	    main()

1	"""Validate sub-agent responses against production Pydantic schemas + audit checks.
2	
3	Tests:
4	  V1. verifier_response.json parses as VerificationBatch (with model_validator coercion)
5	  V2. all 4 expected claims have a verdict
6	  V3. supporting_evidence ids actually exist in the input
7	  V4. verdicts are in the canonical vocabulary
8	
9	  S1. section_writer_response.txt has [CITE:ev_xxx] markers
10	  S2. all citation IDs reference real evidence_ids from the input
11	  S3. >= 2 unique sources cited
12	  S4. no source cited > 3 times
13	  S5. no CoT leakage markers (I think, Let me, <thinking>, etc.)
14	  S6. ends with terminal punctuation (no truncation)
15	  S7. has Key Findings subsection
16	  S8. word count is sensible (>= 500, <= 2000)
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import json
22	import re
23	import sys
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
27	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
28	import os
29	os.chdir(PROJECT_ROOT)
30	
31	OUT = PROJECT_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "subagent_prompts"
32	
33	
34	def section(name):
35	    print(f"\n{'='*70}\n  {name}\n{'='*70}")
36	
37	
38	def check(name, cond, detail=""):
39	    status = "PASS" if cond else "FAIL"
40	    print(f"  [{status}] {name}{' -- ' + detail if detail else ''}")
41	    return cond
42	
43	
44	def validate_verifier():
45	    section("VERIFIER PROMPT TEST")
46	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import VerificationBatch
47	
48	    inputs = json.loads((OUT / "verifier_inputs.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
49	    raw = (OUT / "verifier_response.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
50	
51	    all_pass = True
52	
53	    # V1: parse
54	    try:
55	        vb = VerificationBatch.model_validate_json(raw)
56	        all_pass &= check("V1. parses as VerificationBatch", True,
57	                          f"{len(vb.verifications)} verifications, faith={vb.overall_faithfulness}")
58	    except Exception as e:
59	        print(f"  parse error: {e}")
60	        all_pass &= check("V1. parses as VerificationBatch", False, str(e)[:120])
61	        return all_pass  # can't continue
62	
63	    # V2: count match
64	    all_pass &= check(
65	        "V2. one verification per input claim",
66	        len(vb.verifications) == len(inputs),
67	        f"got {len(vb.verifications)}, expected {len(inputs)}",
68	    )
69	
70	    # V3: supporting_evidence ids exist
71	    valid_ids = {e["evidence_id"] for e in inputs}
72	    bad_refs = []
73	    for v in vb.verifications:
74	        for ref in v.supporting_evidence:
75	            if ref not in valid_ids:
76	                bad_refs.append(ref)
77	    all_pass &= check(
78	        "V3. supporting_evidence references real evidence_ids",
79	        not bad_refs,
80	        f"unknown refs: {bad_refs[:3]}" if bad_refs else "all known",
81	    )
82	
83	    # V4: verdict vocabulary
84	    canonical = {"SUPPORTED", "PARTIALLY_SUPPORTED", "NOT_SUPPORTED"}
85	    bad_verdicts = [v.verdict for v in vb.verifications if v.verdict not in canonical]
86	    all_pass &= check(
87	        "V4. verdicts use canonical vocabulary",
88	        not bad_verdicts,
89	        f"bad: {bad_verdicts}" if bad_verdicts else "",
90	    )
91	
92	    # V5: confidence in [0, 1]
93	    bad_conf = [v.confidence for v in vb.verifications if not (0.0 <= v.confidence <= 1.0)]
94	    all_pass &= check(
95	        "V5. confidence in [0,1]",
96	        not bad_conf,
97	        f"bad: {bad_conf}" if bad_conf else "",
98	    )
99	
100	    # Print verdict summary
101	    print(f"\n  Verdict summary:")
102	    for i, v in enumerate(vb.verifications):
103	        stored = inputs[i].get("stored_verdict", "?")
104	        print(f"    {i+1}. {v.verdict} (conf={v.confidence:.2f}) -- stored as {stored}")
105	
106	    return all_pass
107	
108	
109	def validate_section_writer():
110	    section("SECTION WRITER PROMPT TEST")
111	    inputs = json.loads((OUT / "section_writer_inputs.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
112	    text = (OUT / "section_writer_response.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
113	
114	    all_pass = True
115	    valid_ids = {e["evidence_id"] for e in inputs}
116	    source_for_id = {e["evidence_id"]: e["source_title"] for e in inputs}
117	
118	    # S1: citations present
119	    cite_pattern = re.compile(r"\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]")
120	    citations = cite_pattern.findall(text)
121	    all_pass &= check(
122	        "S1. has [CITE:ev_xxx] markers",
123	        len(citations) > 0,
124	        f"{len(citations)} citations",
125	    )
126	
127	    # S2: all cited ids exist
128	    bad_cites = [c for c in citations if c not in valid_ids]
129	    all_pass &= check(
130	        "S2. citations reference real evidence_ids",
131	        not bad_cites,
132	        f"unknown: {bad_cites[:3]}" if bad_cites else "all known",
133	    )
134	
135	    # S3: >= 2 unique sources
136	    cited_sources = {source_for_id[c] for c in citations if c in source_for_id}
137	    all_pass &= check(
138	        "S3. >= 2 unique sources cited",
139	        len(cited_sources) >= 2,
140	        f"{len(cited_sources)} unique sources",
141	    )
142	
143	    # S4: no source > 3 times
144	    from collections import Counter
145	    src_counts = Counter(source_for_id[c] for c in citations if c in source_for_id)
146	    over = {s: n for s, n in src_counts.items() if n > 3}
147	    all_pass &= check(
148	        "S4. no source cited > 3 times",
149	        not over,
150	        f"over: {dict(list(over.items())[:2])}" if over else "",
151	    )
152	
153	    # S5: no CoT markers
154	    cot_patterns = [
155	        r"\bI (?:think|will|need to|should)\b",
156	        r"\bLet me\b",
157	        r"<think(?:ing)?>",
158	        r"\bMy approach\b",
159	        r"\bFirst,? I\b",
160	        r"\bHere'?s? (?:my|the) (?:plan|outline|response)\b",
161	        r"\bAs an (?:AI|LLM|assistant)\b",
162	        r"```",  # code fences
163	    ]
164	    cot_hits = []
165	    for p in cot_patterns:
166	        m = re.search(p, text, re.IGNORECASE)
167	        if m:
168	            cot_hits.append(f"{p!r}: {m.group(0)!r}")
169	    all_pass &= check(
170	        "S5. no CoT/scaffolding markers",
171	        not cot_hits,
172	        f"{len(cot_hits)} hits: {cot_hits[0]}" if cot_hits else "",
173	    )
174	
175	    # S6: ends with terminal punctuation
176	    last_chars = text.rstrip()[-3:]
177	    all_pass &= check(
178	        "S6. ends with terminal punctuation",
179	        text.rstrip().endswith((".", "?", "!", ")", '"')),
180	        f"last chars: {last_chars!r}",
181	    )
182	
183	    # S7: Key Findings subsection
184	    has_kf = bool(re.search(r"\*\*Key Findings\*\*|^#+\s*Key Findings", text, re.MULTILINE))
185	    all_pass &= check(
186	        "S7. has Key Findings subsection",
187	        has_kf,
188	    )
189	
190	    # S8: word count sensible
191	    wc = len(text.split())
192	    all_pass &= check(
193	        "S8. word count in [500, 2000]",
194	        500 <= wc <= 2000,
195	        f"{wc} words",
196	    )
197	
198	    # Bonus: detect repeated section title
199	    sec_title = "Mechanisms and Metabolic Effects of Intermittent Fasting"
200	    title_repeated = text.count(sec_title) >= 1
201	    if title_repeated:
202	        print(f"  [WARN] section title appears {text.count(sec_title)}x in body (rule: should NOT repeat)")
203	
204	    return all_pass
205	
206	
207	def main():
208	    if not (OUT / "verifier_response.json").exists():
209	        print(f"MISSING: {OUT / 'verifier_response.json'}")
210	        return 2
211	    if not (OUT / "section_writer_response.txt").exists():
212	        print(f"MISSING: {OUT / 'section_writer_response.txt'}")
213	        return 2
214	
215	    v_pass = validate_verifier()
216	    s_pass = validate_section_writer()
217	
218	    section("SUMMARY")
219	    print(f"  Verifier prompt: {'PASS' if v_pass else 'FAIL'}")
220	    print(f"  Section writer prompt: {'PASS' if s_pass else 'FAIL'}")
221	    return 0 if (v_pass and s_pass) else 1
222	
223	
224	if __name__ == "__main__":
225	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""
2	PG_TEST_053: Evidence Funnel Collapse Prevention
3	
4	Validates FIX-057 (T052 root cause elimination):
5	- Cut 1: Section cap tightened for <10 evidence (max(3, evidence_count))
6	- Cut 2: PG_TIER_SILVER_THRESHOLD 0.40 -> 0.35
7	- Cut 3: Synthesis fallback to GOLD+SILVER confirmed active
8	- Cut 4: PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES 150 -> 180
9	
10	Also validates FIX-053 through FIX-056 from prior session.
11	
12	Key validation targets (compared to T047 benchmarks):
13	1. Evidence after analysis: >= 100 (T052 had 56)
14	2. Evidence after dedup: >= 20 (T052 had 3)
15	3. Sections <= evidence count (T052 had 13 sections for 3 evidence)
16	4. Words >= 10,000 (T052 had 2,091)
17	5. Citations >= 100 (T052 had 13)
18	6. Sources >= 15 (T052 had 3)
19	7. Faithfulness >= 70%

139	        print(f"  GOLD:                 {gold_count} ({gold_count / max(ev_count, 1) * 100:.1f}%)", flush=True)
140	        print(f"  SILVER:               {silver_count} ({silver_count / max(ev_count, 1) * 100:.1f}%)", flush=True)
141	        print(f"  BRONZE:               {bronze_count} ({bronze_count / max(ev_count, 1) * 100:.1f}%)", flush=True)
142	        print(f"  JUNK:                 {junk_count} ({junk_count / max(ev_count, 1) * 100:.1f}%)", flush=True)
143	
144	        # Composite score distribution
145	        composites = [e.get("tier_composite_score", 0) for e in evidence if "tier_composite_score" in e]
146	        if composites:

148	            above_silver = sum(1 for c in composites if c >= 0.35)
149	            above_gold = sum(1 for c in composites if c >= 0.65)
150	            print(f"  Composite avg:        {avg_comp:.4f}", flush=True)
151	            print(f"  Above SILVER (0.35):  {above_silver}/{len(composites)}", flush=True)
152	            print(f"  Above GOLD (0.65):    {above_gold}/{len(composites)}", flush=True)
153	
154	        # FIX-058D: Check Signal 5 default impact
155	        ev_without_nli = [e for e in evidence if "nli_self_check_score" not in e]
156	        ev_with_nli = [e for e in evidence if "nli_self_check_score" in e]
157	        print(f"  [FIX-058D] Evidence WITHOUT nli_self_check_score: {len(ev_without_nli)}/{ev_count} (used default={os.getenv('PG_TIER_SIGNAL5_DEFAULT', '0.3')})", flush=True)
158	        print(f"  [FIX-058D] Evidence WITH nli_self_check_score:    {len(ev_with_nli)}/{ev_count}", flush=True)
159	
160	        # Faithfulness
161	        faithful_claims = [c for c in claims if c.get("is_faithful")]
162	        faith_score = qm.get("faithfulness_score", -1.0)
163	        print(f"  Faithful claims:      {len(faithful_claims)}/{len(claims)}", flush=True)
164	        print(f"  Faithfulness score:   {faith_score:.1%}", flush=True)
165	        print(flush=True)
166	
167	        # --- Quality Metrics ---
168	        print("  --- Quality Metrics ---", flush=True)
169	        total_words = qm.get("total_words", 0)
170	        total_citations = qm.get("total_citations", 0)
171	        unique_sources = qm.get("unique_sources", 0)
172	        print(f"  Words:          {total_words}", flush=True)
173	        print(f"  Sections:       {qm.get('total_sections', 0)}", flush=True)
174	        print(f"  Citations:      {total_citations}", flush=True)
175	        print(f"  Unique sources: {unique_sources}", flush=True)
176	        print(f"  Faithfulness:   {faith_score:.1%}", flush=True)
177	        print(f"  Citation HHI:   {qm.get('citation_hhi', 0):.4f} ({qm.get('citation_hhi_label', '?')})", flush=True)
178	        print(flush=True)
179	
180	        # --- LLM Usage ---
181	        print("  --- LLM Usage ---", flush=True)
182	        cost = usage.get("total_cost_usd", 0)
183	        print(f"  Total calls:    {usage.get('total_calls', 0)}", flush=True)
184	        print(f"  Cost:           ${cost:.4f}", flush=True)
185	        print(flush=True)
186	
187	        # --- NLI Feedback Loop Audit ---
188	        print("  " + "=" * 66, flush=True)
189	        print("  NLI FEEDBACK LOOP AUDIT", flush=True)
190	        print("  " + "=" * 66, flush=True)
191	
192	        ev_nli_scores = [e["nli_self_check_score"] for e in ev_with_nli]
193	        nli_enriched_pct = len(ev_with_nli) / max(len(evidence), 1) * 100
194	        print(f"  [S5] Evidence with nli_self_check_score: {len(ev_with_nli)}/{len(evidence)} ({nli_enriched_pct:.1f}%)", flush=True)
195	        if ev_nli_scores:
196	            avg_nli = sum(ev_nli_scores) / len(ev_nli_scores)
197	            print(f"  [S5] Score range: {min(ev_nli_scores):.3f} - {max(ev_nli_scores):.3f} (avg {avg_nli:.3f})", flush=True)
198	
199	        claims_vtype_dist = Counter(c.get("verification_type", "MISSING") for c in claims)
200	        print(f"  [VC] verification_type distribution: {dict(claims_vtype_dist)}", flush=True)
201	        print(flush=True)
202	
203	        # --- Source Quality ---
204	        print("  --- Source & Report Quality ---", flush=True)
205	        ev_domains = {}
206	        for e in evidence:
207	            url = e.get("source_url", "")
208	            if url:
209	                domain = urlparse(url).netloc or url
210	                ev_domains[domain] = ev_domains.get(domain, 0) + 1
211	        top_domains = sorted(ev_domains.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])[:8]
212	        print(f"  Source domains:           {len(ev_domains)} unique", flush=True)
213	        print(f"  Top domains:              {top_domains}", flush=True)
214	
215	        double_bracket_count = len(re.findall(r'\[\[', report))
216	        phantom_count = report.count("[*]")
217	        print(f"  Double brackets:          {double_bracket_count} (target: 0)", flush=True)
218	        print(f"  Phantom [*]:              {phantom_count} (target: 0)", flush=True)
219

222	        print(f"  Halluc audit:             {len(halluc_audit)} sections, {halluc_rewritten} rewritten", flush=True)
223	        print(f"  [FIX-058A] LettuceDetect disabled: {len(halluc_audit) == 0} (expect True)", flush=True)
224	        print(flush=True)
225	
226	        # --- COMPARISON TABLE ---
227	        dur = elapsed / 60
228	        print("  " + "=" * 150, flush=True)
229	        print("  PG_TEST COMPARISON", flush=True)
230	        print("  " + "=" * 150, flush=True)
231	        header = f"  {'Metric':<22} {'T039':>8} {'T047':>8} {'T052':>8} {'T058':>8}"
232	        separator = f"  {'-'*22} {'-'*8} {'-'*8} {'-'*8} {'-'*8}"
233	        print(header, flush=True)
234	        print(separator, flush=True)
235	        print(f"  {'Faithfulness':<22} {'80.5%':>8} {'100%':>8} {'N/A':>8} {faith_score:>7.1%}", flush=True)
236	        print(f"  {'Duration (min)':<22} {'84':>8} {'105':>8} {'370+':>8} {dur:>8.0f}", flush=True)
237	        cost_str = f"${cost:.2f}"
238	        print(f"  {'Cost':<22} {'$1.31':>8} {'$0.72':>8} {'$0.55':>8} {cost_str:>8}", flush=True)
239	        print(f"  {'Words':<22} {'11583':>8} {'12375':>8} {'2091':>8} {total_words:>8}", flush=True)
240	        print(f"  {'Citations':<22} {'191':>8} {'243':>8} {'13':>8} {total_citations:>8}", flush=True)
241	        print(f"  {'Sources':<22} {'18':>8} {'40':>8} {'3':>8} {unique_sources:>8}", flush=True)
242	        print(f"  {'Evidence':<22} {'1011':>8} {'258':>8} {'56':>8} {ev_count:>8}", flush=True)
243	        print(f"  {'GOLD+SILVER':<22} {'N/A':>8} {'N/A':>8} {'?':>8} {gold_count + silver_count:>8}", flush=True)
244	        print("  " + "=" * 150, flush=True)
245	
246	        # Save result
247	        OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
248	        out_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}.json"
249	        with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
250	            json.dump(result, f, indent=2, default=str)
251	        print(f"\n  Result saved: {out_path}", flush=True)
252	
253	        if report:
254	            report_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}_report.md"
255	            with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
256	                f.write(report)
257	            print(f"  Report saved: {report_path}", flush=True)
258	
259	        # PASS/FAIL
260	        print(flush=True)
261	        hard_fails = []
262	        soft_warns = []
263	
264	        # Hard fails
265	        faith_sentinel = (faith_score == -1.0)
266	        if faith_sentinel:
267	            hard_fails.append("Faithfulness = -1.0 sentinel (never measured)")
268	        if faith_score >= 0 and faith_score < 0.50:
269	            hard_fails.append(f"Faithfulness {faith_score:.1%} < 50%")
270	        if total_words < 6000:
271	            hard_fails.append(f"Words {total_words} < 6,000 (target: 10,000)")
272	        if total_citations < 30:
273	            hard_fails.append(f"Citations {total_citations} < 30")
274	        if unique_sources < 10:
275	            hard_fails.append(f"Sources {unique_sources} < 10 (target: 15)")
276	        if phantom_count > 0:
277	            hard_fails.append(f"Phantom [*] markers found: {phantom_count}")
278	
279	        # Evidence funnel check
280	        if ev_count < 20:
281	            hard_fails.append(f"Evidence funnel COLLAPSED: only {ev_count} pieces (target >= 100)")
282	        if gold_count + silver_count < 10:
283	            hard_fails.append(f"GOLD+SILVER only {gold_count + silver_count} (target >= 10)")
284	
285	        # Soft warnings
286	        if faith_score >= 0 and faith_score < 0.75:
287	            soft_warns.append(f"Faithfulness {faith_score:.1%} below 75% target")
288	        if total_words < 10000:
289	            soft_warns.append(f"Words {total_words} below 10,000 T047 benchmark")
290	        if total_citations < 100:
291	            soft_warns.append(f"Citations {total_citations} below 100 T047 benchmark")
292	        if unique_sources < 20:
293	            soft_warns.append(f"Sources {unique_sources} below 20 T047 benchmark")
294	        if cost > 5.0:
295	            soft_warns.append(f"Cost ${cost:.2f} exceeds $5.00 target")
296	        if len(halluc_audit) > 0:
297	            soft_warns.append(f"LettuceDetect ran despite being disabled ({len(halluc_audit)} sections)")
298	
299	        if hard_fails:
300	            print("  *** HARD FAIL ***", flush=True)
301	            for f in hard_fails:
302	                print(f"    [X] {f}", flush=True)
303	        if soft_warns:
304	            print("  *** WARNINGS ***", flush=True)
305	            for w in soft_warns:
306	                print(f"    [!] {w}", flush=True)
307	
308	        if not hard_fails:
309	            print(f"  *** PG_TEST_058: PASS ***", flush=True)
310	        else:
311	            print(f"  *** PG_TEST_058: FAIL ({len(hard_fails)} hard fails) ***", flush=True)
312	
313	    except Exception as e:
314	        elapsed = time.time() - start
315	        logger.exception("PG_TEST_058 FAILED with exception")
316	        print(f"\n  *** PG_TEST_058 FAILED ({elapsed:.0f}s): {e}", flush=True)
317	        raise
318	
319	
320	if __name__ == "__main__":
321	    asyncio.run(main())

1	"""PG_TEST_060: Full Production Pipeline — Validate 26 New Trace Emissions."""
2

32	
33	async def main():
34	    from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
35	
36	    print("=" * 70, flush=True)
37	    print("  PG_TEST_060: FULL PRODUCTION PIPELINE VALIDATION", flush=True)
38	    print("  Validating 26 new trace emissions + dashboard rendering", flush=True)
39	    print("=" * 70, flush=True)

41	    start = time.time()
42	
43	    try:
44	        result = await build_and_run(
45	            vector_id=VECTOR_ID,
46	            query=(
47	                "What are the most effective and affordable water filtration "
48	                "technologies for removing PFAS from drinking water?"
49	            ),
50	            application="Household Water Filter",
51	            region="NORTH AMERICA",
52	            stage=1,
53	            max_iterations=int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_ITERATIONS", "5")),
54	            max_execution_minutes=int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES", "180")),
55	        )

60	        report = result.get("final_report", "")
61	        evidence = result.get("evidence_chain", result.get("evidence", []))
62	
63	        print(flush=True)
64	        print("=" * 70, flush=True)
65	        print(f"  PG_TEST_060 COMPLETED ({elapsed:.0f}s / {elapsed / 60:.1f}min)", flush=True)

68	        print(f"  Citations: {qm.get('total_citations', 0)}", flush=True)

70	        print(f"  Faithfulness: {qm.get('faithfulness_pct', 0):.1f}%", flush=True)

83	            with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
84	                f.write(report)
85	            print(f"  Report saved: {report_path}", flush=True)
86	
87	        # Validate trace file exists and has new event types
88	        trace_path = Path(f"logs/pg_trace_{VECTOR_ID}.jsonl")
89	        if trace_path.exists():
90	            event_types = set()
91	            evidence_actions = set()
92	            total_events = 0
93	            with open(trace_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
94	                for line in f:
95	                    total_events += 1
96	                    try:
97	                        evt = json.loads(line)
98	                        event_types.add(evt.get("event_type", ""))
99	                        if evt.get("event_type") == "evidence":

101	                    except json.JSONDecodeError:

105	            print(f"  Event types: {sorted(event_types)}", flush=True)
106	            print(f"  Evidence actions: {sorted(evidence_actions)}", flush=True)
107	
108	            # Check for required new emissions

110	            required_actions = {
111	                "tier_scoring_detail", "dedup_detail", "verification_context",
112	                "citation_mapping_full", "expansion_detail",
113	            }
114	            missing_types = required_types - event_types
115	            missing_actions = required_actions - evidence_actions
116	            if missing_types:
117	                print(f"\n  *** MISSING event types: {missing_types}", flush=True)
118	            if missing_actions:
119	                print(f"  *** MISSING evidence actions: {missing_actions}", flush=True)
120	            if not missing_types and not missing_actions:
121	                print("\n  ALL required new emissions PRESENT", flush=True)
122	        else:
123	            print(f"\n  *** WARNING: Trace file not found: {trace_path}", flush=True)
124	
125	    except Exception as e:
126	        elapsed = time.time() - start
127	        logger.exception("PG_TEST_060 FAILED")
128	        print(f"\n  *** PG_TEST_060 FAILED ({elapsed:.0f}s): {e}", flush=True)
129	        raise
130	
131	
132	if __name__ == "__main__":
133	    asyncio.run(main())

1	"""PG_TEST_061: Full Production Pipeline — Post-Session-29 Quality Validation.
2	
3	Validates:
4	1. Blocked-domain veto rule (new) — BRONZE for commercial/blocked sources
5	2. 5-signal tier scoring — GOLD/SILVER/BRONZE distribution
6	3. Evidence quality gates — substance veto, quote validation
7	4. End-to-end report quality — faithfulness, citations, word count
8	5. Trace emissions — all required event types present
9	"""
10	
11	import asyncio
12	import json
13	import logging
14	import os
15	import sys
16	import time
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	from dotenv import load_dotenv
20	
21	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
23	os.chdir(PROJECT_ROOT)
24	
25	load_dotenv()
26	
27	logging.basicConfig(
28	    level=logging.INFO,
29	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(name)s | %(message)s",
30	    handlers=[
31	        logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout),
32	        logging.FileHandler("logs/polaris_graph.log", mode="a", encoding="utf-8"),
33	    ],
34	)
35	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
36	
37	VECTOR_ID = "PG_TEST_090"
38	OUTPUT_DIR = Path("outputs/polaris_graph")
39	
40	
41	async def main():
42	    from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
43	
44	    print("=" * 70, flush=True)
45	    print("  PG_TEST_061: POST-SESSION-29 QUALITY VALIDATION", flush=True)
46	    print("  Focus: tier scoring, blocked-domain veto, report quality", flush=True)
47	    print("=" * 70, flush=True)
48	
49	    start = time.time()
50	
51	    try:
52	        result = await build_and_run(
53	            vector_id=VECTOR_ID,
54	            query=(
55	                "What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent "
56	                "fasting based on clinical research and meta-analyses?"
57	            ),
58	            application="Evidence-Based Health Research",
59	            region="GLOBAL",
60	            stage=1,
61	            max_iterations=int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_ITERATIONS", "2")),
62	            max_execution_minutes=int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_EXECUTION_MINUTES", "150")),
63	        )
64	
65	        elapsed = time.time() - start
66	        qm = result.get("quality_metrics") or {}
67	        usage = result.get("llm_usage") or {}
68	        report = result.get("final_report", "")
69	        evidence = result.get("evidence_chain", result.get("evidence", []))
70	        sections = result.get("sections", result.get("report_sections", []))
71	        bibliography = result.get("bibliography", [])
72	
73	        # --- Quality Metrics ---
74	        total_words = qm.get("total_words", 0)
75	        total_citations = qm.get("total_citations", 0)
76	        unique_sources = qm.get("unique_sources", 0)
77	        faithfulness_pct = qm.get("faithfulness_pct", qm.get("faithfulness_score", 0) * 100)
78	        cost = usage.get("total_cost_usd", 0)
79	
80	        print(flush=True)
81	        print("=" * 70, flush=True)
82	        print(f"  PG_TEST_061 COMPLETED ({elapsed:.0f}s / {elapsed / 60:.1f}min)", flush=True)
83	        print(f"  Evidence: {len(evidence)}", flush=True)
84	        print(f"  Words: {total_words}", flush=True)
85	        print(f"  Citations: {total_citations}", flush=True)
86	        print(f"  Unique Sources: {unique_sources}", flush=True)
87	        print(f"  Faithfulness: {faithfulness_pct:.1f}%", flush=True)
88	        print(f"  Sections: {len(sections)}", flush=True)
89	        print(f"  Bibliography: {len(bibliography)}", flush=True)
90	        print(f"  Cost: ${cost:.2f}", flush=True)
91	        print("=" * 70, flush=True)
92	
93	        # --- Tier Distribution ---
94	        tier_counts = {"GOLD": 0, "SILVER": 0, "BRONZE": 0, "UNVERIFIED": 0}
95	        for ev in evidence:
96	            tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "UNVERIFIED")
97	            tier_counts[tier] = tier_counts.get(tier, 0) + 1
98	        print(f"\n  Tier Distribution: {tier_counts}", flush=True)
99	
100	        # --- Blocked-Domain Veto Check ---
101	        blocked_gold_silver = []
102	        for ev in evidence:
103	            url = ev.get("source_url", "")
104	            tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "")
105	            veto = ev.get("veto_reason", "")
106	            if veto == "blocked_domain_zero_authority" and tier in ("GOLD", "SILVER"):
107	                blocked_gold_silver.append(url)
108	        if blocked_gold_silver:
109	            print(f"\n  *** VETO FAILURE: Blocked domains in GOLD/SILVER: {blocked_gold_silver}", flush=True)
110	        else:
111	            print("  Blocked-domain veto: OK (no blocked sources above BRONZE)", flush=True)
112	
113	        # --- Substance Veto Check ---
114	        substance_vetoed = sum(1 for ev in evidence if "substance" in ev.get("veto_reason", ""))
115	        print(f"  Substance vetoes: {substance_vetoed}", flush=True)
116	
117	        # --- Quality Gates ---
118	        gates_passed = 0
119	        gates_total = 6
120	        gate_results = []
121	
122	        # Gate 1: Word count >= 2000
123	        g1 = total_words >= 2000
124	        gates_passed += int(g1)
125	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g1 else 'FAIL'}] Words >= 2000: {total_words}")
126	
127	        # Gate 2: Citations >= 5
128	        g2 = total_citations >= 5
129	        gates_passed += int(g2)
130	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g2 else 'FAIL'}] Citations >= 5: {total_citations}")
131	
132	        # Gate 3: Unique sources >= 5
133	        g3 = unique_sources >= 5
134	        gates_passed += int(g3)
135	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g3 else 'FAIL'}] Sources >= 5: {unique_sources}")
136	
137	        # Gate 4: Faithfulness >= 70%
138	        g4 = faithfulness_pct >= 70.0
139	        gates_passed += int(g4)
140	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g4 else 'FAIL'}] Faithfulness >= 70%: {faithfulness_pct:.1f}%")
141	
142	        # Gate 5: Evidence count > 0
143	        g5 = len(evidence) > 0
144	        gates_passed += int(g5)
145	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g5 else 'FAIL'}] Evidence > 0: {len(evidence)}")
146	
147	        # Gate 6: Report non-empty
148	        g6 = len(report) > 100
149	        gates_passed += int(g6)
150	        gate_results.append(f"  [{'PASS' if g6 else 'FAIL'}] Report length > 100: {len(report)}")
151	
152	        print(f"\n  Quality Gates: {gates_passed}/{gates_total}", flush=True)
153	        for gr in gate_results:
154	            print(gr, flush=True)
155	
156	        # --- Save Results ---
157	        OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
158	        out_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}.json"
159	        with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
160	            json.dump(result, f, indent=2, default=str)
161	        print(f"\n  Result saved: {out_path}", flush=True)
162	
163	        if report:
164	            report_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}_report.md"
165	            with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
166	                f.write(report)
167	            print(f"  Report saved: {report_path}", flush=True)
168	
169	        # --- Trace Validation ---
170	        trace_path = Path(f"logs/pg_trace_{VECTOR_ID}.jsonl")
171	        if trace_path.exists():
172	            event_types = set()
173	            evidence_actions = set()
174	            total_events = 0
175	            with open(trace_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
176	                for line in f:
177	                    total_events += 1
178	                    try:
179	                        evt = json.loads(line)
180	                        event_types.add(evt.get("type", evt.get("event_type", "")))
181	                        if evt.get("type", evt.get("event_type")) == "evidence":
182	                            evidence_actions.add(evt.get("data", {}).get("action", ""))
183	                    except json.JSONDecodeError:
184	                        pass
185	
186	            print(f"\n  Trace: {trace_path} ({total_events} events, {trace_path.stat().st_size / 1024:.0f} KB)", flush=True)
187	            print(f"  Event types: {sorted(event_types)}", flush=True)
188	
189	            required_types = {"pipeline_start", "llm_detail"}
190	            missing_types = required_types - event_types
191	            if missing_types:
192	                print(f"  *** MISSING event types: {missing_types}", flush=True)
193	            else:
194	                print("  All required event types PRESENT", flush=True)
195	        else:
196	            print(f"\n  *** WARNING: Trace file not found: {trace_path}", flush=True)
197	
198	        # --- Final Verdict ---
199	        if gates_passed == gates_total:
200	            print(f"\n  {'=' * 50}", flush=True)
201	            print(f"  PG_TEST_061: FULL PASS ({gates_passed}/{gates_total} gates)", flush=True)
202	            print(f"  {'=' * 50}", flush=True)
203	        else:
204	            print(f"\n  {'=' * 50}", flush=True)
205	            print(f"  PG_TEST_061: PARTIAL PASS ({gates_passed}/{gates_total} gates)", flush=True)
206	            print(f"  {'=' * 50}", flush=True)
207	
208	    except Exception as e:
209	        elapsed = time.time() - start
210	        logger.exception("PG_TEST_061 FAILED")
211	        print(f"\n  *** PG_TEST_061 FAILED ({elapsed:.0f}s): {e}", flush=True)
212	        raise
213	
214	
215	if __name__ == "__main__":
216	    asyncio.run(main())
217

1	"""
2	Wiki E2E Test — runs full pipeline with PG_WIKI_ENABLED=1.
3	
4	This is the Phase 1 ship test. It runs the complete graph (plan→search→
5	storm→analyze→verify→evaluate→synthesize) with the wiki synthesizer
6	replacing the old synthesis pipeline.
7	"""
8	
9	import asyncio
10	import json
11	import logging
12	import os
13	import re
14	import sys
15	import time
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	# Force wiki mode
19	os.environ["PG_WIKI_ENABLED"] = "1"
20	
21	from dotenv import load_dotenv
22	load_dotenv()
23	
24	# Ensure wiki stays enabled after load_dotenv
25	os.environ["PG_WIKI_ENABLED"] = "1"
26	
27	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
28	
29	logging.basicConfig(
30	    level=logging.INFO,
31	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(message)s",
32	)
33	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
34	
35	VECTOR_ID = "PG_WIKI_004"
36	QUERY = "What are the proven health benefits and risks of intermittent fasting based on clinical evidence?"
37	APPLICATION = "Intermittent Fasting"
38	REGION = "global"
39	OUTPUT_DIR = Path("outputs/polaris_graph")
40	
41	
42	async def main():
43	    start = time.time()
44	    logger.info("=" * 60)
45	    logger.info("POLARIS Wiki E2E Test")
46	    logger.info("PG_WIKI_ENABLED=%s", os.getenv("PG_WIKI_ENABLED"))
47	    logger.info("=" * 60)
48	
49	    from src.polaris_graph.graph import build_and_run
50	
51	    result = await build_and_run(
52	        vector_id=VECTOR_ID,
53	        query=QUERY,
54	        application=APPLICATION,
55	        region=REGION,
56	        stage=1,
57	        max_iterations=2,
58	        max_execution_minutes=60,
59	    )
60	
61	    elapsed = time.time() - start
62	
63	    # ── Validate output ──────────────────────────────────────────
64	    report = result.get("final_report", "")
65	    sections = result.get("sections", [])
66	    bibliography = result.get("bibliography", [])
67	    quality = result.get("quality_metrics", {})
68	
69	    total_words = quality.get("total_words", 0)
70	    total_citations = quality.get("total_citations", 0)
71	    unique_sources = quality.get("unique_sources", 0)
72	    zero_cite = quality.get("zero_cite_sections", 0)
73	
74	    logger.info("\n" + "=" * 60)
75	    logger.info("RESULTS")
76	    logger.info("=" * 60)
77	    logger.info("Status: %s", result.get("status", "unknown"))
78	    logger.info("Sections: %d", len(sections))
79	    logger.info("Words: %d", total_words)
80	    logger.info("Citations: %d", total_citations)
81	    logger.info("Sources: %d", unique_sources)
82	    logger.info("Zero-cite sections: %d", zero_cite)
83	    logger.info("Quality gate: %s", quality.get("quality_gate_result", result.get("quality_gate_result", "?")))
84	    logger.info("Time: %.1f min", elapsed / 60)
85	    logger.info("Cost: $%.4f", result.get("llm_usage", {}).get("total_cost_usd", 0))
86	
87	    # Per-section breakdown
88	    logger.info("\nPer-section:")
89	    for s in sections:
90	        cites = len(s.get("citation_ids", []))
91	        logger.info("  %s: %dw, %d cites — %s",
92	                     s["section_id"], s["word_count"], cites, s["title"][:50])
93	
94	    # Check wiki exists
95	    wiki_path = Path("wiki") / VECTOR_ID
96	    if wiki_path.exists():
97	        wiki_files = list(wiki_path.rglob("*.md"))
98	        logger.info("\nWiki: %s (%d files)", wiki_path, len(wiki_files))
99	    else:
100	        logger.warning("\nWiki directory NOT created at %s", wiki_path)
101	
102	    # ── Gate checks ──────────────────────────────────────────────
103	    gates = []
104	    if total_words < 2000:
105	        gates.append(f"FAIL: words={total_words}<2000")
106	    if total_citations < 5:
107	        gates.append(f"FAIL: citations={total_citations}<5")
108	    if unique_sources < 5:
109	        gates.append(f"FAIL: sources={unique_sources}<5")
110	    if zero_cite > 0:
111	        gates.append(f"FAIL: {zero_cite} zero-cite sections")
112	    if not report:
113	        gates.append("FAIL: empty report")
114	
115	    # Check no [CITE:ev_xxx] markers remain
116	    cite_markers = len(re.findall(r"\[CITE:", report))
117	    if cite_markers:
118	        gates.append(f"FAIL: {cite_markers} [CITE:] markers in report")
119	
120	    if gates:
121	        logger.warning("\nGATE FAILURES:")
122	        for g in gates:
123	            logger.warning("  %s", g)
124	    else:
125	        logger.info("\nALL GATES PASSED")
126	
127	    # ── Save output ──────────────────────────────────────────────
128	    OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
129	    output_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}.json"
130	    report_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{VECTOR_ID}_report.md"
131	
132	    with open(output_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
133	        json.dump(result, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)
134	    with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
135	        f.write(report)
136	
137	    logger.info("\nOutput: %s", output_path)
138	    logger.info("Report: %s", report_path)
139	    logger.info("\nNext: python -u scripts/eval_geval.py %s", output_path)
140	
141	
142	if __name__ == "__main__":
143	    asyncio.run(main())
