1	"""
2	Phase 0B: Wiki Composition Proof-of-Concept
3	
4	Takes TEST_090's existing evidence, groups by topic similarity to outline sections,
5	composes each section from ONLY the assigned claims with pre-resolved citations,
6	assembles a report, and runs G-Eval.
7	
8	This proves whether structured composition (wiki pattern) improves faithfulness
9	over the current pipeline architecture — WITHOUT building any infrastructure.
10	
11	Cost: ~$1 (10 compose calls + G-Eval). Time: ~15 min.
12	"""
13	
14	import asyncio
15	import json
16	import logging
17	import os
18	import re
19	import sys
20	import time
21	from pathlib import Path
22	
23	from dotenv import load_dotenv
24	
25	load_dotenv()
26	
27	# Add project root to path
28	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent))
29	
30	from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient
31	
32	logging.basicConfig(
33	    level=logging.INFO,
34	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(message)s",
35	)
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	SOURCE_FILE = "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_090.json"
39	OUTPUT_FILE = "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_090_wiki_proof.json"
40	REPORT_FILE = "outputs/polaris_graph/PG_TEST_090_wiki_proof_report.md"
41	
42	
43	def group_evidence_by_section(evidence: list[dict], sections: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
44	    """
45	    Group evidence by section using keyword overlap (no embeddings needed).
46	    Each evidence piece goes to the section whose title+description best matches
47	    the evidence statement. Evidence can go to multiple sections if relevant.
48	    """
49	    section_evidence: dict[str, list[dict]] = {s["section_id"]: [] for s in sections}
50	
51	    for ev in evidence:
52	        statement = ev.get("statement", "").lower()
53	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "").lower()
54	        text = f"{statement} {quote}"
55	
56	        # Score each section by keyword overlap
57	        scores = []
58	        for sec in sections:
59	            title = sec.get("title", "").lower()
60	            desc = sec.get("description", "").lower()
61	            keywords = set(re.findall(r'\b[a-z]{4,}\b', f"{title} {desc}"))
62	            evidence_words = set(re.findall(r'\b[a-z]{4,}\b', text))
63	            overlap = len(keywords & evidence_words)
64	            scores.append((sec["section_id"], overlap))
65	
66	        # Assign to top 2 matching sections (evidence can be relevant to multiple)
67	        scores.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
68	        for sid, score in scores[:2]:
69	            if score >= 2:  # At least 2 keyword overlaps
70	                section_evidence[sid].append(ev)
71	
72	    # Log distribution
73	    for sid, evs in section_evidence.items():
74	        sec_title = next((s["title"] for s in sections if s["section_id"] == sid), "?")
75	        logger.info(f"  {sid}: {len(evs)} evidence → {sec_title[:50]}")
76	
77	    return section_evidence
78	
79	
80	def format_claims_for_compose(evidence: list[dict]) -> tuple[str, list[dict]]:
81	    """
82	    Format evidence as numbered claims with pre-resolved citations.
83	    Returns (formatted_text, bibliography_entries).
84	    """
85	    # Dedup by source URL, keeping best evidence per source
86	    by_url: dict[str, list[dict]] = {}
87	    for ev in evidence:
88	        url = ev.get("source_url", "")
89	        if url not in by_url:
90	            by_url[url] = []
91	        by_url[url].append(ev)
92	
93	    # Build bibliography (one entry per unique source)
94	    bibliography = []
95	    url_to_refnum = {}
96	    for i, (url, evs) in enumerate(by_url.items(), 1):
97	        best = max(evs, key=lambda e: e.get("relevance_score", 0))
98	        bibliography.append({
99	            "ref_num": i,
100	            "url": url,
101	            "title": best.get("source_title", "Unknown"),
102	            "authors": best.get("authors", []),
103	            "year": best.get("year"),
104	            "doi": best.get("doi"),
105	        })
106	        url_to_refnum[url] = i
107	
108	    # Format claims with [REF:N]
109	    lines = []
110	    for ev in evidence:
111	        ref_num = url_to_refnum.get(ev.get("source_url", ""), 0)
112	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
113	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
114	        if statement and ref_num:
115	            lines.append(f"- CLAIM: {statement} [REF:{ref_num}]")
116	            if quote and quote != statement:
117	                lines.append(f"  QUOTE: \"{quote[:200]}\"")
118	
119	    return "\n".join(lines), bibliography
120	
121	
122	COMPOSE_SYSTEM = """You are a senior academic researcher writing a section of a systematic review.
123	
124	RULES:
125	1. Write ONLY from the CLAIMS provided below. Do not add facts, statistics, or findings not in the claims.
126	2. Every factual statement MUST include its [REF:N] citation from the claims.
127	3. You MAY add interpretive commentary ("These findings suggest...", "Taken together...") WITHOUT citations — but only for synthesis, never for new facts.
128	4. Write in third person, academic register.
129	5. Include a "**Key Findings**" section at the end with 3-5 bullet points summarizing the most important claims.
130	6. Target 800-1500 words.
131	7. Do NOT use "Evidence suggests that" as a sentence opener.
132	8. Do NOT include chain-of-thought, planning, or meta-commentary about the writing process.
133	"""
134	
135	
136	async def compose_section(
137	    client: OpenRouterClient,
138	    section_title: str,
139	    section_description: str,
140	    claims_text: str,
141	    section_order: int,
142	    total_sections: int,
143	    prev_summary: str = "",
144	) -> str:
145	    """Compose one section from pre-cited claims."""
146	    context_block = ""
147	    if prev_summary:
148	        context_block = f"\nPREVIOUS SECTIONS COVERED:\n{prev_summary}\nDo NOT repeat these points.\n"
149	
150	    prompt = f"""Write section {section_order}/{total_sections}: "{section_title}"
151	
152	Section focus: {section_description}
153	{context_block}
154	CLAIMS (use ONLY these, cite with [REF:N]):
155	{claims_text}
156	
157	Write the section now. Every factual claim must have [REF:N]. Target 800-1500 words."""
158	
159	    result = await client.generate(
160	        prompt=prompt,
161	        system=COMPOSE_SYSTEM,
162	        max_tokens=4096,
163	        temperature=0.3,
164	    )
165	    return result.content
166	
167	
168	async def run_proof():
169	    """Main proof-of-concept execution."""
170	    logger.info("=" * 60)
171	    logger.info("Phase 0B: Wiki Composition Proof-of-Concept")
172	    logger.info("=" * 60)
173	
174	    # Load TEST_090 data
175	    with open(SOURCE_FILE) as f:
176	        data = json.load(f)
177	
178	    evidence = data["evidence"]
179	    outline = data["section_outline"]
180	    query = data["original_query"]
181	
182	    logger.info(f"Query: {query[:80]}")
183	    logger.info(f"Evidence: {len(evidence)} pieces ({sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get('quality_tier') == 'GOLD')} GOLD, "
184	                f"{sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get('quality_tier') == 'SILVER')} SILVER)")
185	    logger.info(f"Sections: {len(outline)}")
186	
187	    # Filter to GOLD + SILVER only
188	    quality_evidence = [e for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") in ("GOLD", "SILVER")]
189	    logger.info(f"After quality filter: {len(quality_evidence)} evidence (GOLD+SILVER only)")
190	
191	    # Group evidence by section
192	    logger.info("\nGrouping evidence by section (keyword overlap):")
193	    section_evidence = group_evidence_by_section(quality_evidence, outline)
194	
195	    # Initialize LLM client
196	    client = OpenRouterClient()
197	
198	    # Compose each section sequentially
199	    sections = []
200	    all_bibliography = []
201	    prev_summary = ""
202	    start_time = time.time()
203	
204	    for i, sec in enumerate(outline):
205	        sid = sec["section_id"]
206	        title = sec["title"]
207	        desc = sec.get("description", title)
208	        evs = section_evidence.get(sid, [])
209	
210	        if not evs:
211	            logger.warning(f"Section {sid} '{title[:40]}' has 0 evidence — skipping")
212	            continue
213	
214	        claims_text, bib = format_claims_for_compose(evs)
215	        all_bibliography.extend(bib)
216	
217	        logger.info(f"\nComposing {sid}: '{title[:50]}' ({len(evs)} evidence, {len(bib)} sources)")
218	
219	        try:
220	            content = await compose_section(
221	                client=client,
222	                section_title=title,
223	                section_description=desc,
224	                claims_text=claims_text,
225	                section_order=i + 1,
226	                total_sections=len(outline),
227	                prev_summary=prev_summary,
228	            )
229	        except (ValueError, Exception) as exc:
230	            logger.warning(f"Section {sid} compose failed: {exc} — skipping")
231	            continue
232	
233	        # Convert [REF:N] to [N] (deterministic)
234	        content = re.sub(r'\[REF:(\d+)\]', r'[\1]', content)
235	
236	        # Count citations
237	        cite_count = len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', content))
238	        word_count = len(content.split())
239	
240	        sections.append({
241	            "section_id": sid,
242	            "title": title,
243	            "content": content,
244	            "word_count": word_count,
245	            "citation_count": cite_count,
246	            "evidence_ids": [e.get("evidence_id", "") for e in evs],
247	        })
248	
249	        logger.info(f"  → {word_count} words, {cite_count} citations")
250	
251	        # Build summary for next section
252	        # Extract first 2 sentences as summary
253	        sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', content)
254	        prev_summary += f"- {title}: {' '.join(sentences[:2])}\n"
255	
256	    elapsed = time.time() - start_time
257	
258	    # Dedup bibliography by URL
259	    seen_urls = set()
260	    unique_bib = []
261	    for b in all_bibliography:
262	        if b["url"] not in seen_urls:
263	            seen_urls.add(b["url"])
264	            unique_bib.append(b)
265	
266	    # Re-number bibliography
267	    for i, b in enumerate(unique_bib, 1):
268	        b["ref_num"] = i
269	
270	    # Assemble report
271	    total_words = sum(s["word_count"] for s in sections)
272	    total_cites = sum(s["citation_count"] for s in sections)
273	
274	    report_md = f"# {query}\n\n"
275	    for s in sections:
276	        report_md += f"## {s['title']}\n\n{s['content']}\n\n"
277	    report_md += "## References\n\n"
278	    for b in unique_bib:
279	        authors = ", ".join(b.get("authors", [])[:3]) if b.get("authors") else "Unknown"
280	        year = b.get("year", "n.d.")
281	        report_md += f"[{b['ref_num']}] {authors} ({year}). {b['title'][:80]}. {b['url']}\n\n"
282	
283	    # Build output JSON (G-Eval compatible)
284	    output = {
285	        "vector_id": "PG_TEST_090_WIKI_PROOF",
286	        "original_query": query,
287	        "sections": sections,
288	        "final_report": report_md,
289	        "bibliography": unique_bib,
290	        "quality_metrics": {
291	            "total_words": total_words,
292	            "total_sections": len(sections),
293	            "total_citations": total_cites,
294	            "unique_sources": len(unique_bib),
295	            "faithfulness_score": None,  # To be filled by G-Eval
296	            "total_evidence": len(quality_evidence),
297	            "gold_evidence": sum(1 for e in quality_evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD"),
298	            "silver_evidence": sum(1 for e in quality_evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "SILVER"),
299	        },
300	        "evidence": quality_evidence,
301	        "llm_usage": {
302	            "total_cost_usd": client.usage.total_cost_usd,
303	            "total_calls": client.usage.total_calls,
304	        },
305	    }
306	
307	    # Save
308	    Path(OUTPUT_FILE).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
309	    with open(OUTPUT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
310	        json.dump(output, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)
311	    with open(REPORT_FILE, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
312	        f.write(report_md)
313	
314	    # Print summary
315	    logger.info("\n" + "=" * 60)
316	    logger.info("PHASE 0B RESULTS")
317	    logger.info("=" * 60)
318	    logger.info(f"Sections composed: {len(sections)}/{len(outline)}")
319	    logger.info(f"Total words: {total_words}")
320	    logger.info(f"Total citations: {total_cites}")
321	    logger.info(f"Unique sources: {len(unique_bib)}")
322	    logger.info(f"Cost: ${client.usage.total_cost_usd:.4f}")
323	    logger.info(f"Time: {elapsed:.1f}s ({elapsed/60:.1f} min)")
324	    logger.info(f"Output: {OUTPUT_FILE}")
325	    logger.info(f"Report: {REPORT_FILE}")
326	
327	    # Per-section summary
328	    logger.info("\nPer-section breakdown:")
329	    for s in sections:
330	        logger.info(f"  {s['section_id']}: {s['word_count']}w, {s['citation_count']} cites — {s['title'][:50]}")
331	
332	    # Compare with TEST_090
333	    logger.info("\nComparison with TEST_090:")
334	    logger.info(f"  TEST_090: {data['quality_metrics']['total_words']}w, "
335	                f"{sum(s.get('citation_count', len(re.findall(r'\\[\\d+\\]', s.get('content','')))) for s in data['sections'])} total cites, "
336	                f"30 unique sources")
337	    logger.info(f"  WIKI_PROOF: {total_words}w, {total_cites} total cites, {len(unique_bib)} unique sources")
338	
339	    zero_cite_sections = sum(1 for s in sections if s["citation_count"] == 0)
340	    logger.info(f"  TEST_090 zero-cite sections: 3")
341	    logger.info(f"  WIKI_PROOF zero-cite sections: {zero_cite_sections}")
342	
343	    logger.info(f"\nNext: Run G-Eval → python -u scripts/eval_geval.py {OUTPUT_FILE}")
344	
345	    return output
346	
347	
348	if __name__ == "__main__":
349	    asyncio.run(run_proof())
350

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""DICED preflight harness (I-deepfix-001 #1344): run every pipeline DICE in ISOLATION
3	against a banked drb_72 corpus_snapshot fixture and assert its §-1.3 / §-1.4 invariant
4	FAIL-LOUD, then print a per-stage GREEN/RED table and a final GO / NO-GO.
5	
6	WHY THIS EXISTS
7	---------------
8	A preflight that GREENs on a known-bad fixture is useless (§-1.4 false-green guard). This
9	harness is built and CALIBRATED so that, on the CURRENT (pre-breadth-fix) banked drb_72
10	artifacts, it goes RED exactly on the stages the recent eruptions hit:
11	
12	  * BREADTH under-surface  -> D2_composition_breadth  (cited-distinct / generator-pool < floor)
13	                              D3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget (the upstream front-half reduction:
14	                              the B4 relevance gate + fetch budget -- RED on the OLD banked manifest
15	                              that predates the A5c relevance_gate demote-not-drop disclosure)
16	  * REPETITION (forced floor padding) -> D2_composition_repetition (n-gram repeat-rate ceiling)
17	  * FALSE-CONTRADICTION     -> D3_contradiction_only_comparable (headline count vs comparable)
18	
19	...while the HONEST stages stay GREEN (selection dropped==0, consolidation baskets form,
20	strict_verify is the only drop, render is clean by the INDEPENDENT detector, the pipeline-
21	verdict gates do not thin sources). A blanket-RED harness would be just as useless as a
22	blanket-GREEN one.
23	
24	I-deepfix-001 #1344 adds 5 LATENT GUARD-GAP dice the stage map surfaced (each GREEN where the
25	stage was healthy on the banked fixture, RED where the gap is real):
26	  * D4_relevance_weight_not_drop   -- relevance WEIGHTS not DROPS (structural keep-all; weight
27	                                      floor LIVE)           [GREEN on banked: dropped_count==0]
28	  * D5_credibility_honest_tiering  -- credibility is honest WEIGHT-tiering, every source
29	                                      classified              [GREEN on banked: 149==149, weaker
30	                                      tiering_mode invariant deferred to LIVE]
31	  * D6_topic_gate_env_guard        -- the §-1.3-banned scope hard-filter escape hatch is unset
32	                                                              [GREEN on banked: env unset]
33	  * D1_consolidation_qualitative_basket -- consolidation forms a QUALITATIVE (non-numeric) basket
34	                                      [KNOWN-RED-BY-DESIGN on the current numeric-only fact_dedup;
35	                                      exposes the blind spot the behavioral fix closes post-commit
36	                                      -- it is NOT weakened to pass, so the gate stays NO-GO until
37	                                      a real qualitative basket forms]
38	  * FX06_population_coupling        -- adequacy(D7) population == approval(D8) distribution total
39	                                                              [GREEN on banked: 149==149==149]
40	And it RE-ATTRIBUTES D3 (see ``dice_d3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget``): the 760-source pre-fetch
41	reduction is the B4 RELEVANCE GATE + fetch BUDGET, not the (OFF) prefetch off-topic filter.
42	
43	ARCHITECTURE (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER / CONSOLIDATE-not-DROP / BASKET-FAITHFULNESS)
44	----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
45	The ONLY sanctioned DROP in the whole pipeline is ``strict_verify``. Every other stage is a
46	WEIGHT (relevance / credibility / topic / selection-reorder) or a CONSOLIDATION (finding/fact
47	dedup -> corroboration baskets) or a pipeline-VERDICT gate (scope / adequacy / approval, which
48	abort the WHOLE question, never thin sources). Each DICE below asserts the slice of that law it
49	owns, in isolation, on the fixture.
50	
51	OFFLINE-DETERMINISTIC dice are real assertions over the banked JSON checkpoints + report.md
52	(``load the stage's JSON checkpoint``) or over the actual stage function on a controlled fixture
53	(D6 calls the real ``report_redactor.reconcile_report_against_verdicts``; D7 shells out to the
54	INDEPENDENT clean-room render detector ``scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py`` -- NOT the
55	BLIND production chrome predicate, per I-wire-013).
56	
57	LIVE-ONLY dice (4-role judge 429-storm / seam tear; GPU OOM) cannot be validated by a banked
58	replay (the I-wire-013/014 false-PASS lesson: a banked replay structurally cannot validate a
59	live-transport completion fix or a fetch-side truncation fix). Those emit a clearly-labelled
60	``LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED`` line with the exact tiny command to run that dice live. This harness
61	NEVER makes a paid call.
62	
63	LAW VI: every threshold + path is an env var / CLI arg (``PG_DICED_*``). No magic numbers.
64	LAW II / §8.4: no network, no model load, no heavy import in the default offline path. The only
65	src import is the stdlib-only ``report_redactor`` module, loaded by file path so the heavy
66	``src/polaris_graph`` package __init__ never runs.
67	
68	Usage
69	-----
70	    python scripts/pipeline_diced_preflight.py                 # default drb_72 fixture
71	    python scripts/pipeline_diced_preflight.py --fixture DIR   # repeatable; search order
72	    python scripts/pipeline_diced_preflight.py --json out.json # machine-readable sidecar
73	
74	Exit code: 0 == GO (no offline dice RED), 1 == NO-GO (>=1 offline dice RED), 2 == harness error.
75	"""

116	def _env_float(name: str, default: float) -> float:
117	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
118	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
119	        return default
120	    return float(raw)

123	def _env_int(name: str, default: int) -> int:
124	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
125	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
126	        return default
127	    return int(raw)

131	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
132	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
133	        return default

137	def _env_list(name: str, default: list[str]) -> list[str]:
138	    raw = os.environ.get(name)
139	    if raw is None or raw.strip() == "":
140	        return list(default)
141	    return [p.strip() for p in raw.split(",") if p.strip()]

174	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# Fixture resolver -- search the roots in order; fail loud on a required-but-absent artifact.
176	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	class FixtureResolver:
179	    def __init__(self, roots: list[Path]):
180	        self.roots = [Path(r) for r in roots]
181	        live = [r for r in self.roots if r.is_dir()]
182	        if not live:
183	            raise FileNotFoundError(
184	                "no fixture directory exists among: " + ", ".join(str(r) for r in self.roots)
185	            )
186	        self.roots = live
187	
188	    def find(self, name: str) -> Optional[Path]:
189	        # honor a per-artifact override env var, e.g. PG_DICED_REPORT for report.md
190	        ov = os.environ.get("PG_DICED_" + name.replace(".", "_").replace("-", "_").upper())
191	        if ov:
192	            p = Path(ov)
193	            return p if p.exists() else None
194	        for r in self.roots:
195	            p = r / name
196	            if p.exists():
197	                return p
198	        return None
199	
200	    def require(self, name: str) -> Path:
201	        p = self.find(name)
202	        if p is None:
203	            raise FileNotFoundError(
204	                f"required fixture artifact '{name}' not found in any root: "
205	                + ", ".join(str(r) for r in self.roots)
206	            )
207	        return p
208	
209	    def load_json(self, name: str) -> object:
210	        return json.loads(self.require(name).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
211	
212	    def read_text(self, name: str) -> str:
213	        return self.require(name).read_text(encoding="utf-8")

244	def _normalize_prose(text: str) -> str:
245	    text = re.sub(r"\[#ev:[^\]]+\]", " ", text)   # strip provenance tokens
246	    text = re.sub(r"\[\d+\]", " ", text)          # strip [N] citation markers
247	    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9 ]", " ", text.lower())
248	    return re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()
249	
250	
251	def _ngram_repetition(text: str, n: int) -> tuple[float, int, str]:
252	    """Return (repeat_rate, max_phrase_count, top_phrase). repeat_rate = 1 - distinct/total."""
253	    words = _normalize_prose(text).split()
254	    if len(words) <= n:
255	        return 0.0, 0, ""
256	    grams = [" ".join(words[i:i + n]) for i in range(len(words) - n + 1)]
257	    counts = Counter(grams)
258	    total = len(grams)
259	    distinct = len(counts)
260	    repeat_rate = 1.0 - (distinct / total) if total else 0.0
261	    top_phrase, top_count = counts.most_common(1)[0]
262	    return repeat_rate, top_count, top_phrase
263	
264	
265	def _distinct_citation_keys(report_text: str) -> int:
266	    return len(set(re.findall(r"\[(\d+)\]", report_text)))
267

269	def _generator_pool_size(manifest: dict) -> int:
270	    sel = manifest.get("evidence_selection", {}) or {}
271	    for key in ("evidence_selected", "evidence_total"):
272	        v = sel.get(key)
273	        if isinstance(v, int) and v > 0:
274	            return v
275	    # fall back to the corpus count if selection counts are absent
276	    return int((manifest.get("corpus", {}) or {}).get("count", 0))

286	def dice_d0_scope_no_source_drop(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
287	    inv = "scope is a question-level accept/reject verdict, never a per-source thinner"
288	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
289	    status = m.get("status", "")

297	def dice_d1_retrieval_reconcile(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
298	    inv = "fetched + failed reconciles to candidates_processed; the funnel is fully accounted"
299	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
300	    r = m.get("retrieval", {}) or {}
301	    fetched = int(r.get("fetched", 0))
302	    failed = int(r.get("failed", 0))
303	    processed = int(r.get("candidates_processed", 0))
304	    ok = (fetched + failed) == processed and processed > 0
305	    detail = f"fetched={fetched} + failed={failed} == candidates_processed={processed} -> {ok}"
306	    return DiceResult("D1_retrieval_reconcile", "front", "OFFLINE",
307	                      GREEN if ok else RED, inv, detail)

310	def dice_d3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
311	    """RE-ATTRIBUTED (I-deepfix-001 #1344): the drb_72 760-source pre-fetch reduction is the B4
312	    RELEVANCE GATE (PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR ordering) + the disclosed PG_LIVE_FETCH_CAP fetch BUDGET --
313	    NOT the prefetch off-topic filter, which was OFF on this run (prefetch_offtopic=None,
314	    kept_by_offtopic=926, dropped ZERO). The old dice blamed the off-topic filter and gated on
315	    `dropped_pre_fetch==0`, mis-labelling the drop. The §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER proof lives in the
316	    A5c-landed manifest field `retrieval.relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill` (below-floor
317	    candidates the BUDGET still FETCHED -> proof the floor only ORDERS, never hard-cuts). On a
318	    FRESH (B4-ON) manifest the dice is GREEN when that demote-not-drop disclosure is present
319	    (demoted_fetched_to_fill>0) and the only bound is the disclosed fetch budget; on the OLD banked
320	    manifest (no relevance_gate key) it stays RED -- honest, pre-fix."""
321	    inv = ("the pre-fetch reduction is the B4 relevance gate (PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR) WEIGHT-demotion "
322	           "+ disclosed fetch budget, NOT a hard filter: off-topic filter drops 0, "
323	           "relevance_gate.demoted_fetched_to_fill>0 (demote-not-drop), budget is the only bound")
324	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
325	    r = m.get("retrieval", {}) or {}
326	    caps = r.get("retrieval_caps", {}) or {}
327	    dropped = int(caps.get("dropped_pre_fetch", 0))
328	    discovered = int(caps.get("candidates_discovered", 0))
329	
330	    # (1) the off-topic filter is NOT the dropper -- it is a SEPARATE weight, OFF on drb_72.
331	    # prefetch_offtopic is None (filter off) or a disclosure dict; pull its hard-drop count if any.
332	    pf = r.get("prefetch_offtopic")
333	    if isinstance(pf, dict):
334	        offtopic_dropped = int(pf.get("rejected", pf.get("dropped", 0)) or 0)
335	    else:
336	        offtopic_dropped = 0
337	    kept_by_offtopic = r.get("kept_by_offtopic")
338	    offtopic_ok = offtopic_dropped <= ctx.th.prefetch_max_drop
339	
340	    # (2) the disclosed fetch BUDGET cap (the §-1.3 cost bound that the drop must reflect).
341	    truncs = caps.get("search_truncations", []) or []
342	    budget_caps = {c.upper() for c in ctx.th.fetch_budget_caps}
343	    budget_entry = next(
344	        (st for st in truncs if str(st.get("cap", "")).upper() in budget_caps), None)
345	    budget_disclosed = budget_entry is not None
346	
347	    # (3) the A5c DEMOTE-NOT-DROP disclosure (None on the OLD banked manifest => RED, honest).
348	    rg = r.get("relevance_gate")
349	    has_rg = isinstance(rg, dict)
350	    demoted_below_floor = int(rg.get("demoted_below_floor", 0)) if has_rg else 0
351	    demoted_fetched_to_fill = int(rg.get("demoted_fetched_to_fill", 0)) if has_rg else 0
352	    demoted_tail = int(rg.get("demoted_tail", 0)) if has_rg else 0
353	    unfetched_relevant_tail = int(rg.get("unfetched_relevant_tail", 0)) if has_rg else 0
354	    # Robust weight-not-filter proof (the dropped_pre_fetch funnel = discovered-fetched-failed is a
355	    # DIFFERENT count from the gate's scored pool -- seeds bypass scoring -- so a strict equality is
356	    # unsafe; the budget-only attribution is proven by (a) the demote disclosure existing,
357	    # (b) demoted_fetched_to_fill>0, and (c) the disclosed budget being the bound). The exact
358	    # arithmetic (gate tail vs dropped_pre_fetch) is surfaced advisory + confirmed by LIVE smoke.
359	    weight_to_fill = demoted_fetched_to_fill >= ctx.th.min_demoted_fetched_to_fill
360	    gate_tail = unfetched_relevant_tail + demoted_tail
361	    ok = has_rg and weight_to_fill and offtopic_ok and budget_disclosed
362	
363	    budget_str = (f"{budget_entry.get('cap')}={budget_entry.get('value')} bit={budget_entry.get('bit')}"
364	                  if budget_entry else "NONE-DISCLOSED")
365	    if has_rg:
366	        detail = (f"dropped_pre_fetch={dropped} of discovered={discovered}; off-topic filter "
367	                  f"dropped={offtopic_dropped} (kept_by_offtopic={kept_by_offtopic}, NOT the dropper); "
368	                  f"fetch_budget[{budget_str}]; relevance_gate demote-not-drop: below_floor="
369	                  f"{demoted_below_floor} fetched_to_fill={demoted_fetched_to_fill} "
370	                  f"tail={demoted_tail} unfetched_relevant_tail={unfetched_relevant_tail} "
371	                  f"(gate_tail={gate_tail}); fetched_to_fill>=min({ctx.th.min_demoted_fetched_to_fill}) "
372	                  f"-> {'WEIGHT-not-FILTER (budget-only)' if ok else 'NOT PROVEN'}")
373	    else:
374	        detail = (f"dropped_pre_fetch={dropped} of discovered={discovered}; off-topic filter "
375	                  f"dropped={offtopic_dropped} (prefetch_offtopic={pf!r}, kept_by_offtopic="
376	                  f"{kept_by_offtopic}) so it is NOT the dropper; fetch_budget[{budget_str}]; "
377	                  f"retrieval.relevance_gate ABSENT (B4 OFF / pre-A5c banked manifest) -> the demote-"
378	                  f"not-drop proof (demoted_fetched_to_fill) cannot be read -> the {dropped}-drop is "
379	                  f"the B4 relevance gate + fetch budget but UN-disclosed-as-weight -> RED (honest pre-fix)")
380	    return DiceResult("D3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget", "front", "OFFLINE",
381	                      GREEN if ok else RED, inv, detail)
382

421	def dice_d4_judge_completed_offline(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
422	    inv = "4-role judge COMPLETED: final_verdicts non-empty, not abort_role_transport_exhausted"
423	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
424	    fr = m.get("four_role_evaluation", {}) or {}
425	    fv = fr.get("final_verdicts", {}) or {}
426	    held = fr.get("held_reasons")
427	    status = m.get("status", "")
428	    transport_dead = status == "abort_role_transport_exhausted"
429	    ok = bool(fv) and not transport_dead

533	def _load_report_redactor():
534	    """Load the stdlib-only report_redactor module by file path (no heavy package __init__)."""
535	    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location(
536	        "polaris_report_redactor_isolated", str(_REPORT_REDACTOR_PATH)
537	    )
538	    if spec is None or spec.loader is None:
539	        raise ImportError(f"cannot build spec for {_REPORT_REDACTOR_PATH}")
540	    mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
541	    # Register BEFORE exec: @dataclass resolves sys.modules[cls.__module__].__dict__, which is
542	    # None for an unregistered synthetic module (Py3.12+ dataclasses fail-loud otherwise).
543	    sys.modules[spec.name] = mod
544	    spec.loader.exec_module(mod)
545	    return mod

654	def dice_d4_relevance_weight_not_drop(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:

657	    content_relevance=None) -> assert the structural keep-all (dropped_count==0) and defer the
658	    per-passage min-weight>0 floor to a LIVE smoke. A real relevance hard-drop (dropped_count>0)
659	    flips this RED."""
660	    inv = ("relevance leg WEIGHTS-not-DROPS: selection kept-all (dropped_count==0); per-passage "
661	           "min relevance weight>0 is LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED when the banked fixture lacks weights")

669	    cr = r.get("content_relevance")
670	    weights: list[float] = []
671	    if isinstance(cr, dict):
672	        raw_w = cr.get("passage_weights") or cr.get("weights") or []
673	        if isinstance(raw_w, list):
674	            weights = [float(w) for w in raw_w if isinstance(w, (int, float))]
675	    keep_all = (dropped == 0)
676	    if weights:
677	        min_w = min(weights)
678	        ok = keep_all and min_w > 0.0
679	        detail = (f"per-passage relevance weights n={len(weights)} min_weight={min_w:.4f} "
680	                  f"dropped_count={dropped} -> {'WEIGHT-not-DROP' if ok else 'DROP/zero-weight'}")
681	    else:
682	        ok = keep_all
683	        detail = (f"dropped_count={dropped} strategy={strategy!r} semantic_relevance_fell_back="
684	                  f"{fell_back}; banked fixture has NO per-passage relevance weights -> structural "
685	                  f"keep-all asserted; LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED: min relevance weight>0 on a fresh "
686	                  f"PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1 run -> {'KEEP-ALL OK' if ok else 'RELEVANCE DROP'}")
687	    return DiceResult("D4_relevance_weight_not_drop", "front", "OFFLINE",

691	def dice_d5_credibility_honest_tiering(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
692	    """GAP: credibility/tier is an honest WEIGHT over ALL sources (§-1.3), never a degraded
693	    rules-floor that silently drops. Strong invariant (when a fresh manifest carries the NESTED
694	    `corpus_credibility_disclosure.tiering_status.tiering_mode`): mode != rules_floor_degraded AND
695	    every source classified (0 dropped). The banked manifest lacks `tiering_status.tiering_mode`
696	    -> assert the weaker invariant (disclosure present + classified count == sources) and emit a
697	    LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED note for tiering_mode. A classified!=sources mismatch (a drop) or a degraded
698	    tiering mode flips this RED."""
699	    inv = ("credibility is honest WEIGHT-tiering: tiering_mode != rules_floor_degraded AND every "
700	           "source classified (classified count == total_sources, 0 dropped)")
701	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
702	    ccd = m.get("corpus_credibility_disclosure", {}) or {}
703	    tier_counts = ccd.get("tier_counts", {}) or {}
704	    classified = sum(int(v) for v in tier_counts.values())
705	    total_sources = ccd.get("total_sources")
706	    corpus_count = int((m.get("corpus", {}) or {}).get("count", 0))
707	    status_present = bool(ccd) and bool(ccd.get("gate") or ccd.get("disclosure_note"))
708	    count_ok = (total_sources is not None and classified == total_sources
709	                and total_sources == corpus_count)
710	    # D5 wiring (#1344 wave-3) serializes the batch tiering status NESTED under the disclosure as
711	    # corpus_credibility_disclosure.tiering_status.tiering_mode (weighted_corpus_gate.disclosure_to_dict
712	    # -> asdict of CorpusCredibilityDisclosure.tiering_status). Read the NESTED path -- the old TOP-level
713	    # ccd.get("tiering_mode") was ALWAYS None, so the strong invariant never fired even on a fresh ON run.
714	    # Defensive: tiering_status is a dict on a fresh ON run, None on the OFF path / replay, and ABSENT on
715	    # the banked fixture -> all three collapse to {} so we fall through to the weaker classified-count
716	    # invariant when no real tiering_mode is present.
717	    tiering_status = ccd.get("tiering_status")
718	    tiering_status = tiering_status if isinstance(tiering_status, dict) else {}
719	    tiering_mode = tiering_status.get("tiering_mode")
720	    disclosed_gap = m.get("credibility_disclosed_gap")
721	    if tiering_mode is not None:
722	        ok = status_present and count_ok and tiering_mode != ctx.th.degraded_tiering_mode
723	        detail = (f"tiering_mode={tiering_mode!r} (!= {ctx.th.degraded_tiering_mode!r}); classified="
724	                  f"{classified} total_sources={total_sources} corpus.count={corpus_count} "
725	                  f"status_present={status_present} -> {'HONEST TIERING' if ok else 'DEGRADED/DROP'}")
726	    else:
727	        ok = status_present and count_ok
728	        detail = (f"banked manifest lacks tiering_status.tiering_mode -> WEAKER invariant: status_present="
729	                  f"{status_present} classified={classified}==total_sources={total_sources}=="
730	                  f"corpus.count={corpus_count} -> {'KEEP-ALL OK' if ok else 'COUNT MISMATCH/DROP'}; "
731	                  f"credibility_disclosed_gap={str(disclosed_gap)[:60]!r}; LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED: "
732	                  f"tiering_mode != {ctx.th.degraded_tiering_mode!r} + credibility-pass completes "
733	                  f"in-wall on a fresh run (the banked scoring pass timed out, disclosed)")
734	    return DiceResult("D5_credibility_honest_tiering", "front", "OFFLINE",
735	                      GREEN if ok else RED, inv, detail)

774	def dice_d1_consolidation_qualitative_basket(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
775	    """GAP (KNOWN-RED, exposing): consolidation must CONSOLIDATE qualitative claims too (§-1.3), not
776	    numeric-only. The current fact_dedup/finding_dedup keys every corroboration basket on a NUMERIC
777	    finding_key slot, so a non-numeric (qualitative) claim can never form a multi-source basket.
778	    This dice asserts >=1 corroborated QUALITATIVE basket exists -- EXPECTED to be RED on the banked
779	    fixture (it exposes the numeric-only blind spot the behavioral fix will close post-commit). It is
780	    NOT weakened to pass; it goes GREEN only once a real qualitative basket forms."""
781	    inv = ("consolidation forms a corroboration basket for >=1 QUALITATIVE (non-numeric) claim "
782	           "(CONSOLIDATE qualitative too, §-1.3), not numeric-only")
783	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
784	    clusters = (m.get("finding_dedup", {}) or {}).get("clusters", []) or []
785	
786	    def _has_numeric_key(finding_key) -> bool:
787	        if not isinstance(finding_key, (list, tuple)):
788	            return False
789	        for el in finding_key:
790	            if isinstance(el, bool):
791	                continue
792	            if isinstance(el, (int, float)) and float(el) != 0.0:
793	                return True
794	        return False
795	
796	    corro = [c for c in clusters if int(c.get("corroboration_count", 0)) > 1]
797	    qual_corro = [c for c in corro if not _has_numeric_key(c.get("finding_key", []))]
798	    n_qual = len(qual_corro)
799	    ok = n_qual >= ctx.th.min_qualitative_baskets
800	    detail = (f"corroborated_baskets(>1)={len(corro)} of which QUALITATIVE(non-numeric finding_key)="
801	              f"{n_qual} (min {ctx.th.min_qualitative_baskets}); fact_dedup keys baskets on a numeric "
802	              f"finding_key slot ONLY -> the qualitative-consolidation BLIND SPOT "
803	              f"-> {'OK' if ok else 'KNOWN-RED-BY-DESIGN (exposes the gap; the behavioral fix closes it)'}")
804	    return DiceResult("D1_consolidation_qualitative_basket", "back", "OFFLINE",
805	                      GREEN if ok else RED, inv, detail, by_design=not ok)
806	
807	
808	def dice_fx06_population_coupling(ctx: Ctx) -> DiceResult:
809	    """GAP (FX-06): D7<->D8 self-consistency. The source population the adequacy gate (D7) evaluated
810	    must equal the approval (D8) tier-distribution total and the disclosed total_sources. The
811	    historic 833-vs-639 eruption was exactly this desync. Strong form reads `adequacy.total_sources`
812	    when a fresh manifest carries it; on the banked fixture (adequacy carries only findings counts)
813	    it falls back to corpus.count as the adequacy-evaluated population. Any mismatch flips RED."""
814	    inv = ("D7<->D8 population coupling: adequacy source population == approval tier-distribution "
815	           "total == disclosed total_sources (the 833-vs-639 desync goes RED)")
816	    m = ctx.fx.load_json("manifest.json")
817	    adq = m.get("adequacy", {}) or {}
818	    corpus = m.get("corpus", {}) or {}
819	    ccd = m.get("corpus_credibility_disclosure", {}) or {}
820	    adq_total = adq.get("total_sources")
821	    if adq_total is None:
822	        adq_total = corpus.get("count")
823	        adq_src = "corpus.count (adequacy.total_sources absent on banked manifest)"
824	    else:
825	        adq_src = "adequacy.total_sources"
826	    tier_counts = ccd.get("tier_counts", {}) or {}
827	    approval_dist_total = sum(int(v) for v in tier_counts.values())
828	    ccd_total = ccd.get("total_sources")
829	    ok = (adq_total is not None and approval_dist_total > 0
830	          and adq_total == approval_dist_total == ccd_total)
831	    detail = (f"adequacy_population={adq_total} [{adq_src}] vs approval tier-distribution total="
832	              f"{approval_dist_total} vs disclosed total_sources={ccd_total} "
833	              f"-> {'COUPLED' if ok else 'POPULATION DESYNC (833-vs-639 class)'}")
834	    return DiceResult("FX06_population_coupling", "front", "OFFLINE",
835	                      GREEN if ok else RED, inv, detail)
836	
837	
838	OFFLINE_DICE: list[Callable[[Ctx], DiceResult]] = [
839	    # front-half lane (RETRIEVAL -> SELECTION)
840	    dice_d0_scope_no_source_drop,
841	    dice_d1_retrieval_reconcile,
842	    dice_d3_relevance_gate_fetch_budget,
843	    dice_d4_relevance_weight_not_drop,
844	    dice_d5_credibility_honest_tiering,
845	    dice_d6_topic_gate_env_guard,
846	    dice_d7_adequacy_proceed,
847	    dice_d8_approval_no_drop,
848	    dice_d9_selection_keep_all,
849	    dice_fx06_population_coupling,
850	    # back-half lane (CONSOLIDATION -> RENDER)
851	    dice_d1_consolidation_baskets,
852	    dice_d1_consolidation_qualitative_basket,
853	    dice_d2_composition_breadth,
854	    dice_d2_composition_repetition,
855	    dice_d3_contradiction_only_comparable,
856	    dice_d4_judge_completed_offline,
857	    dice_d5_strict_verify_only_drop,
858	    dice_d6_redaction_backbone_tear,
859	    dice_d7_render_chrome,
860	]
861	
862	
863	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
864	# LIVE-ONLY DICE -- cannot be validated by a banked replay (I-wire-013/014 false-PASS lesson).
865	# Emit an exact tiny command; NEVER make a paid call here. All run on the VM (memory: ALL heavy
866	# GPU+LLM runs on the VM, never local). Placeholders are env-tunable.
867	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
868	
869	def live_dice(fx: FixtureResolver) -> list[DiceResult]:
870	    vm = os.environ.get("PG_DICED_VM_HOST", "<vm-ssh-host>")          # e.g. ssh2.vast.ai -p 37450
871	    snap = os.environ.get("PG_DICED_LIVE_SNAPSHOT", "outputs/<run>/corpus_snapshot.json")
872	    question = os.environ.get("PG_DICED_LIVE_QUESTION", "drb_72")
873	    judge_cmd = (
874	        f"# 4-ROLE JUDGE 429-storm / seam-tear -- a live-transport phenomenon a banked replay "
875	        f"CANNOT reproduce.\n"
876	        f"#   ssh {vm} 'cd /workspace/POLARIS && env -u OPENAI_API_KEY "
877	        f"PG_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_DEADLINE_S=120 python scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py "
878	        f"--only \"{question}\" --resume {snap} --stop-after four_role 2>&1 | "
879	        f"tee /tmp/diced_judge_smoke.log'\n"
880	        f"#   ASSERT: manifest.status != abort_role_transport_exhausted ; "
881	        f"four_role 429 sidecar 429s-seen==0 ; _seam_held_reason is None ; "
882	        f"four_role_claim_audit.json has a row for every settled claim."
883	    )
884	    oom_cmd = (
885	        f"# GPU OOM degrade -- needs the real 2xRTX3090Ti VM GPU; offline cannot load the model.\n"
886	        f"#   ssh {vm} 'cd /workspace/POLARIS && CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0 python -c "
887	        f"\"from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer as S; "
888	        f"m=S(\\\"Qwen/Qwen3-Embedding-8B\\\"); print(m.encode([\\\"probe\\\"]*512).shape)\" "
889	        f"2>&1 | tee /tmp/diced_oom_smoke.log'\n"
890	        f"#   ASSERT: completes OR fails LOUD with a disclosed OOM-degrade "
891	        f"(clinical_pdf_winner_degraded / semantic_relevance_fell_back set + LOUD log), "
892	        f"never a silent empty-embedding."
893	    )
894	    return [
895	        DiceResult("D4_four_role_judge_seam_LIVE", "back", "LIVE", LIVE,
896	                   "judge COMPLETES under live transport: 0 seam_timeout, 0 429-storm",
897	                   "the 429/seam tear is a live-transport phenomenon; a banked replay cannot "
898	                   "validate completion (I-wire-013/014 false-PASS lesson)",
899	                   judge_cmd),
900	        DiceResult("GPU_OOM_degrade_LIVE", "infra", "LIVE", LIVE,
901	                   "GPU OOM degrades LOUD + disclosed, never a silent empty embedding",
902	                   "OOM only reproduces on the real VM GPU under a full batch; offline cannot "
903	                   "load the model (memory: heavy GPU runs on the VM, never local)",
904	                   oom_cmd),
905	    ]
906	
907	
908	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
909	# Runner
910	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
911	
912	def _run_offline(ctx: Ctx) -> list[DiceResult]:
913	    out = []
914	    for fn in OFFLINE_DICE:
915	        try:
916	            out.append(fn(ctx))
917	        except Exception as exc:  # fail-loud: an un-runnable dice is RED, never skipped
918	            tb = traceback.format_exc(limit=3).strip().splitlines()[-1]
919	            out.append(DiceResult(
920	                getattr(fn, "__name__", "unknown").replace("dice_", ""),
921	                "?", "OFFLINE", RED,
922	                "dice could not execute (fail-loud)",
923	                f"EXCEPTION {type(exc).__name__}: {exc} :: {tb}",
924	            ))
925	    return out
926	
927	
928	def _print_table(offline: list[DiceResult], live: list[DiceResult]) -> None:
929	    name_w = max([len(r.name) for r in offline + live] + [20])
930	    print("\n" + "=" * (name_w + 60))
931	    print("  DICED PREFLIGHT -- per-stage invariant table (drb_72 fixture)")
932	    print("=" * (name_w + 60))
933	    print(f"  {'STATUS':<6} {'LANE':<6} {'DICE':<{name_w}}  INVARIANT / DETAIL")
934	    print("-" * (name_w + 60))
935	    for r in offline:
936	        if r.status == GREEN:
937	            mark = "GREEN"
938	        else:
939	            mark = "RED* " if r.by_design else "RED  "   # RED* == KNOWN-RED-BY-DESIGN (exposing dice)
940	        print(f"  {mark:<6} {r.lane:<6} {r.name:<{name_w}}  {r.invariant}")
941	        print(f"  {'':<6} {'':<6} {'':<{name_w}}    -> {r.detail}")
942	    print(f"  (RED* = KNOWN-RED-BY-DESIGN: an exposing dice whose RED is intentional until the "
943	          f"behavioral fix lands; it is NOT a regression.)")
944	    if live:
945	        print("-" * (name_w + 60))
946	        print("  LIVE-SMOKE-REQUIRED (not run here; no paid call):")
947	        for r in live:
948	            print(f"  {LIVE}  {r.lane:<6} {r.name}")
949	            print(f"        invariant: {r.invariant}")
950	            print(f"        why-live : {r.detail}")
951	            for cl in r.live_command.splitlines():
952	                print(f"        {cl}")
953	    print("-" * (name_w + 60))
954	
955	
956	def main(argv: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> int:
957	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="DICED preflight harness for the POLARIS pipeline.")
958	    ap.add_argument("--fixture", action="append", default=None,
959	                    help="fixture root dir (repeatable; searched in order). "
960	                         "Default: the banked drb_72 deepfix_replay_forensic2 + deepfix_run1_audit.")
961	    ap.add_argument("--json", default=None, help="write a machine-readable result sidecar here.")
962	    args = ap.parse_args(argv)
963	
964	    if args.fixture:
965	        roots = [Path(x) for x in args.fixture]
966	    elif os.environ.get("PG_DICED_FIXTURE_DIRS"):
967	        roots = [Path(x) for x in os.environ["PG_DICED_FIXTURE_DIRS"].split(os.pathsep) if x]
968	    else:
969	        roots = list(_DEFAULT_FIXTURE_DIRS)
970	
971	    try:
972	        fx = FixtureResolver(roots)
973	    except FileNotFoundError as exc:
974	        print(f"[diced] HARNESS ERROR: {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
975	        return 2
976	
977	    th = Thresholds()
978	    ctx = Ctx(fx=fx, th=th)
979	
980	    print("[diced] fixture roots (search order):")
981	    for r in fx.roots:
982	        print(f"        - {r}")
983	    print(f"[diced] thresholds: breadth_floor={th.breadth_floor} repetition_ceiling={th.repetition_ceiling} "
984	          f"repetition_ngram={th.repetition_ngram} prefetch_max_drop={th.prefetch_max_drop} "
985	          f"chrome/trunc/contra_max={th.chrome_max}/{th.truncation_max}/{th.contradiction_noise_max} "
986	          f"backbone_min_present={th.backbone_min_present}")
987	    print(f"[diced] guard-gap thresholds: min_demoted_fetched_to_fill={th.min_demoted_fetched_to_fill} "
988	          f"min_qualitative_baskets={th.min_qualitative_baskets} "
989	          f"degraded_tiering_mode={th.degraded_tiering_mode!r} fetch_budget_caps={th.fetch_budget_caps} "
990	          f"topic_gate_hard_drop_env={th.topic_gate_hard_drop_env!r}")
991	
992	    offline = _run_offline(ctx)
993	    live = live_dice(fx)
994	    _print_table(offline, live)
995	
996	    red = [r for r in offline if r.status == RED]
997	    green = [r for r in offline if r.status == GREEN]
998	    red_eruption = [r for r in red if not r.by_design]
999	    red_by_design = [r for r in red if r.by_design]
1000	    verdict = "NO-GO" if red else "GO"
1001	    print(f"\n  OFFLINE RESULT: {len(green)} GREEN, {len(red)} RED "
1002	          f"({len(red_eruption)} eruption-RED + {len(red_by_design)} known-RED-by-design)  ->  {verdict}")
1003	    if red_eruption:
1004	        print("  ERUPTION-RED stages (these PROVE the harness catches the real eruptions):")
1005	        for r in red_eruption:
1006	            print(f"    - {r.name}: {r.detail}")
1007	    if red_by_design:
1008	        print("  KNOWN-RED-BY-DESIGN stages (exposing dice; intentional NO-GO until the fix lands):")
1009	        for r in red_by_design:
1010	            print(f"    - {r.name}: {r.detail}")
1011	    print(f"  LIVE dice needing a smoke (run on the VM, not here): "
1012	          f"{', '.join(r.name for r in live)}")
1013	    print("=" * 80)
1014	
1015	    if args.json:
1016	        payload = {
1017	            "verdict": verdict,
1018	            "fixture_roots": [str(r) for r in fx.roots],
1019	            "thresholds": th.__dict__,
1020	            "offline": [r.__dict__ for r in offline],
1021	            "live": [r.__dict__ for r in live],
1022	            "red_stages": [r.name for r in red],
1023	            "red_eruption_stages": [r.name for r in red_eruption],
1024	            "red_by_design_stages": [r.name for r in red_by_design],
1025	            "green_stages": [r.name for r in green],
1026	        }
1027	        Path(args.json).write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
1028	        print(f"[diced] wrote sidecar -> {args.json}")
1029	
1030	    # Exit non-zero on NO-GO so CI / automation catches a known-bad pre-flight (fail-loud).
1031	    return 1 if red else 0
1032	
1033	
1034	if __name__ == "__main__":

content.text: This file has heavy jargon ("DICED", numbered "dice" functions with arbitrary IDs D0-D9, FX06) but they are internally consistent domain terms for this validation harness, documented thoroughly. Now let me look at the remaining files.

content.content:
1	"""
2	POLARIS Observatory — Playwright Adaptive Layout Audit.
3	
4	Verifies that every tab fills its viewport width intelligently across
5	6 viewports x 6 tabs = 36 combinations. Measures dead space (gap between
6	content bounding box and viewport edge) and reports PASS/WARN/FAIL.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python scripts/playwright_adaptive_audit.py --port 8768
10	    python scripts/playwright_adaptive_audit.py --port 8768 --no-server
11	    python scripts/playwright_adaptive_audit.py --port 8768 --tabs report,advanced
12	"""
13	
14	import argparse
15	import asyncio
16	import json
17	import logging
18	import os
19	import subprocess
20	import sys
21	import time
22	from dataclasses import asdict, dataclass, field
23	from datetime import datetime, timezone
24	from pathlib import Path
25	from typing import Any
26	
27	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
28	# Project root
29	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
30	_PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
31	if str(_PROJECT_ROOT) not in sys.path:
32	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_PROJECT_ROOT))
33	
34	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	# Third-party imports
36	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
37	try:
38	    from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
39	except ImportError:
40	    print("ERROR: playwright not installed. Run: pip install playwright && playwright install")
41	    sys.exit(1)
42	
43	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
44	# Logging
45	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
46	logging.basicConfig(
47	    level=logging.INFO,
48	    format="%(asctime)s [%(levelname)s] %(name)s: %(message)s",
49	)
50	log = logging.getLogger("adaptive_audit")
51	
52	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	# Configuration (LAW VI)
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	DEFAULT_PORT = int(os.getenv("ADAPTIVE_AUDIT_PORT", "8768"))
56	OUTPUT_DIR = Path(os.getenv(
57	    "ADAPTIVE_AUDIT_OUTPUT_DIR",
58	    str(_PROJECT_ROOT / "outputs" / "adaptive_audit"),
59	))
60	SCREENSHOT_DIR = OUTPUT_DIR / "screenshots"
61	
62	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
63	# Viewport matrix
64	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
65	VIEWPORTS: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
66	    {"name": "fullhd", "width": 1920, "height": 1080},
67	    {"name": "laptop", "width": 1440, "height": 900},
68	    {"name": "small_laptop", "width": 1280, "height": 720},
69	    {"name": "tablet_land", "width": 1024, "height": 768},
70	    {"name": "tablet_port", "width": 768, "height": 1024},
71	    {"name": "phone", "width": 375, "height": 812},
72	]
73	
74	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
75	# Tab matrix
76	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
77	TABS: list[str] = [
78	    "research", "evidence", "report", "memory", "pipelines", "advanced",
79	]
80	
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	# Thresholds
83	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	PASS_THRESHOLD_PCT = float(os.getenv("ADAPTIVE_PASS_PCT", "5.0"))
85	WARN_THRESHOLD_PCT = float(os.getenv("ADAPTIVE_WARN_PCT", "15.0"))
86	
87	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
88	# Data classes
89	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
90	
91	@dataclass
92	class ComboResult:
93	    """Result for one tab x viewport combination."""
94	
95	    tab: str
96	    viewport_name: str
97	    viewport_width: int
98	    viewport_height: int
99	    pane_width: float = 0.0
100	    content_width: float = 0.0
101	    dead_space_px: float = 0.0
102	    dead_space_pct: float = 0.0
103	    verdict: str = "SKIP"
104	    screenshot: str = ""
105	    error: str = ""
106	
107	
108	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
109	# Dead-space measurement JS
110	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
111	MEASURE_JS = """
112	() => {
113	    // Find the active view pane
114	    const pane = document.querySelector('.view-pane.active')
115	        || document.querySelector('.view-pane[style*="display: flex"]')
116	        || document.querySelector('.view-pane[style*="display: block"]')
117	        || document.querySelector('.view-pane:not([style*="display: none"])');
118	
119	    if (!pane) {
120	        return { error: 'No active view pane found', paneWidth: 0, containerWidth: 0 };
121	    }
122	
123	    const paneRect = pane.getBoundingClientRect();
124	    const paneWidth = paneRect.width;
125	    const panePadding = parseFloat(window.getComputedStyle(pane).paddingLeft) +
126	        parseFloat(window.getComputedStyle(pane).paddingRight);
127	    const usableWidth = paneWidth - panePadding;
128	
129	    // Strategy: check if any container element has a CSS max-width that is
130	    // smaller than the usable pane width. This detects layout constraints
131	    // regardless of whether content is loaded (empty-state safe).
132	    //
133	    // We walk visible children (and grandchildren) looking for the TIGHTEST
134	    // max-width constraint. If max-width resolves to "none" or a px value
135	    // >= usableWidth, the container is unconstrained. The "effective container
136	    // width" is min(usableWidth, tightestMaxWidth).
137	
138	    // Sidebar/panel classes that are intentionally width-constrained
139	    const SIDEBAR_CLASSES = [
140	        'pipeline-column', 'phase-list-column', 'evidence-detail-panel',
141	        'report-toc', 'storm-sidebar', 'compose-bar', 'compose-drawer',
142	        'nav-bar', 'app-header', 'toast', 'run-query-text',
143	    ];
144	
145	    // Check if an element is a main layout container (not sidebar/inline)
146	    function isLayoutContainer(el, style) {
147	        const display = style.display;
148	        if (display === 'inline' || display === 'inline-block') return false;
149	        // Must have meaningful dimensions
150	        if (el.offsetHeight < 50 && el.offsetWidth < 200) return false;
151	        // Exclude intentionally constrained sidebars/panels
152	        const cls = el.className || '';
153	        for (const sc of SIDEBAR_CLASSES) {
154	            if (cls.includes(sc)) return false;
155	        }
156	        return true;
157	    }
158	
159	    function resolveMaxWidth(el, parentWidth) {
160	        const computed = window.getComputedStyle(el);
161	        const mw = computed.maxWidth;
162	        if (!mw || mw === 'none') return Infinity;
163	        // Already pixels
164	        if (mw.endsWith('px')) return parseFloat(mw);
165	        // Percentage — resolve against parent
166	        if (mw.endsWith('%')) return (parseFloat(mw) / 100) * parentWidth;
167	        // Other units (rem, ch, etc.) — use the element's actual rendered width
168	        // as a proxy since getComputedStyle resolves to px for used values
169	        return parseFloat(mw) || Infinity;
170	    }
171	
172	    let tightest = Infinity;
173	    let constrainedBy = '';
174	    const children = pane.querySelectorAll(':scope > *');
175	
176	    for (const child of children) {
177	        const style = window.getComputedStyle(child);
178	        if (style.display === 'none' || style.visibility === 'hidden') continue;
179	        if (!isLayoutContainer(child, style)) continue;
180	
181	        const childMw = resolveMaxWidth(child, usableWidth);
182	        if (childMw < tightest) {
183	            tightest = childMw;
184	            constrainedBy = child.className || child.tagName;
185	        }
186	
187	        // One level deeper (e.g., .report-view > .report-content)
188	        const grandchildren = child.querySelectorAll(':scope > *');
189	        for (const gc of grandchildren) {
190	            const gcStyle = window.getComputedStyle(gc);
191	            if (gcStyle.display === 'none' || gcStyle.visibility === 'hidden') continue;
192	            if (!isLayoutContainer(gc, gcStyle)) continue;
193	            const gcMw = resolveMaxWidth(gc, child.offsetWidth || usableWidth);
194	            if (gcMw < tightest) {
195	                tightest = gcMw;
196	                constrainedBy = gc.className || gc.tagName;
197	            }
198	        }
199	    }
200	
201	    // Effective container width = min(usableWidth, tightest max-width)
202	    const effectiveWidth = Math.min(usableWidth, tightest === Infinity ? usableWidth : tightest);
203	
204	    return {
205	        paneWidth: paneWidth,
206	        containerWidth: effectiveWidth,
207	        panePadding: panePadding,
208	        usableWidth: usableWidth,
209	        constrainedBy: constrainedBy,
210	        tightestMaxWidthPx: tightest === Infinity ? -1 : tightest,
211	    };
212	}
213	"""
214	
215	
216	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	# Server management
218	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	def _start_server(port: int) -> subprocess.Popen:
221	    """Start the live server as a subprocess, redirect output to log file."""
222	    log_file = OUTPUT_DIR / "server.log"
223	    log_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
224	    fh = open(log_file, "w")
225	    proc = subprocess.Popen(
226	        [sys.executable, str(_PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"),
227	         "--port", str(port)],
228	        stdout=fh,
229	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
230	        cwd=str(_PROJECT_ROOT),
231	    )
232	    log.info("Started live_server.py on port %d (PID %d)", port, proc.pid)
233	    return proc
234	
235	
236	async def _wait_for_server(port: int, timeout: float = 30.0) -> bool:
237	    """Poll health endpoint until it returns 200."""
238	    import urllib.request
239	    import urllib.error
240	    url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}/health"
241	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
242	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
243	        try:
244	            req = urllib.request.urlopen(url, timeout=2)
245	            if req.status == 200:
246	                log.info("Server healthy on port %d", port)
247	                return True
248	        except (urllib.error.URLError, OSError, ConnectionRefusedError):
249	            pass
250	        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)
251	    return False
252	
253	
254	def _stop_server(proc: subprocess.Popen) -> None:
255	    """Terminate the server subprocess."""
256	    if proc.poll() is None:
257	        proc.terminate()
258	        try:
259	            proc.wait(timeout=5)
260	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
261	            proc.kill()
262	        log.info("Server process terminated")
263	
264	
265	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
266	# Core audit
267	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
268	
269	async def _audit_combo(
270	    page,
271	    tab: str,
272	    viewport: dict[str, Any],
273	    port: int,
274	) -> ComboResult:
275	    """Measure dead space for one tab x viewport combination."""
276	    result = ComboResult(
277	        tab=tab,
278	        viewport_name=viewport["name"],
279	        viewport_width=viewport["width"],
280	        viewport_height=viewport["height"],
281	    )
282	
283	    try:
284	        # Set viewport size
285	        await page.set_viewport_size({
286	            "width": viewport["width"],
287	            "height": viewport["height"],
288	        })
289	
290	        # Navigate to base URL (domcontentloaded — SSE connections prevent networkidle)
291	        await page.goto(f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}/", wait_until="domcontentloaded")
292	        await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
293	
294	        # Switch to operator mode and navigate to tab
295	        setup_js = f"setViewMode('operator'); switchView('{tab}')"
296	        await page.evaluate(setup_js)
297	        await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
298	
299	        # Take screenshot
300	        screenshot_name = f"{tab}_{viewport['width']}x{viewport['height']}.png"
301	        screenshot_path = SCREENSHOT_DIR / screenshot_name
302	        await page.screenshot(path=str(screenshot_path), full_page=False)
303	        result.screenshot = screenshot_name
304	
305	        # Measure dead space
306	        metrics = await page.evaluate(MEASURE_JS)
307	
308	        if metrics.get("error"):
309	            result.error = metrics["error"]
310	            result.verdict = "SKIP"
311	            return result
312	
313	        pane_width = metrics["paneWidth"]
314	        container_width = metrics["containerWidth"]
315	        usable_width = metrics["usableWidth"]
316	
317	        # Dead space = usable pane width minus container width
318	        dead_space = max(0, usable_width - container_width)
319	        dead_pct = (dead_space / usable_width * 100) if usable_width > 0 else 0
320	
321	        result.pane_width = round(pane_width, 1)
322	        result.content_width = round(container_width, 1)
323	        result.dead_space_px = round(dead_space, 1)
324	        result.dead_space_pct = round(dead_pct, 1)
325	
326	        if dead_pct > PASS_THRESHOLD_PCT:
327	            constrained = metrics.get("constrainedBy", "unknown")
328	            tightest_px = metrics.get("tightestMaxWidthPx", -1)
329	            log.warning("  Constraint: max-width=%.0fpx on '%s'", tightest_px, constrained)
330	
331	        if dead_pct <= PASS_THRESHOLD_PCT:
332	            result.verdict = "PASS"
333	        elif dead_pct <= WARN_THRESHOLD_PCT:
334	            result.verdict = "WARN"
335	        else:
336	            result.verdict = "FAIL"
337	
338	    except Exception as exc:
339	        result.error = str(exc)
340	        result.verdict = "ERROR"
341	        log.error("Error auditing %s @ %s: %s", tab, viewport["name"], exc)
342	
343	    return result
344	
345	
346	async def run_audit(
347	    port: int,
348	    tabs: list[str],
349	    viewports: list[dict[str, Any]],
350	) -> list[ComboResult]:
351	    """Run the full adaptive audit across all tab x viewport combos."""
352	    results: list[ComboResult] = []
353	
354	    async with async_playwright() as pw:
355	        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
356	        context = await browser.new_context(
357	            viewport={"width": 1920, "height": 1080},
358	            device_scale_factor=1,
359	        )
360	        page = await context.new_page()
361	
362	        total = len(tabs) * len(viewports)
363	        done = 0
364	
365	        for tab in tabs:
366	            for vp in viewports:
367	                done += 1
368	                label = f"[{done}/{total}] {tab} @ {vp['name']} ({vp['width']}x{vp['height']})"
369	                log.info("Auditing %s", label)
370	
371	                result = await _audit_combo(page, tab, vp, port)
372	                results.append(result)
373	
374	                verdict_color = {
375	                    "PASS": "\033[32m",
376	                    "WARN": "\033[33m",
377	                    "FAIL": "\033[31m",
378	                    "ERROR": "\033[31m",
379	                    "SKIP": "\033[90m",
380	                }.get(result.verdict, "")
381	                log.info(
382	                    "  %s%s\033[0m — pane=%.0fpx content=%.0fpx dead=%.0fpx (%.1f%%)",
383	                    verdict_color, result.verdict,
384	                    result.pane_width, result.content_width,
385	                    result.dead_space_px, result.dead_space_pct,
386	                )
387	
388	        await browser.close()
389	
390	    return results
391	
392	
393	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
394	# Reporting
395	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
396	
397	def _print_summary(results: list[ComboResult]) -> int:
398	    """Print a formatted summary table. Returns exit code."""
399	    print("\n" + "=" * 80)
400	    print("  ADAPTIVE LAYOUT AUDIT — SUMMARY")
401	    print("=" * 80)
402	    print(f"  {'Tab':<12} {'Viewport':<14} {'Size':>10} {'Pane':>8} "
403	          f"{'Content':>8} {'Dead':>8} {'%':>6} {'Verdict':>8}")
404	    print("-" * 80)
405	
406	    fail_count = 0
407	    warn_count = 0
408	    pass_count = 0
409	
410	    for r in results:
411	        verdict_str = r.verdict
412	        size_str = f"{r.viewport_width}x{r.viewport_height}"
413	
414	        if r.verdict == "FAIL":
415	            fail_count += 1
416	            marker = "\033[31mFAIL\033[0m"
417	        elif r.verdict == "WARN":
418	            warn_count += 1
419	            marker = "\033[33mWARN\033[0m"
420	        elif r.verdict == "PASS":
421	            pass_count += 1
422	            marker = "\033[32mPASS\033[0m"
423	        elif r.verdict == "ERROR":
424	            fail_count += 1
425	            marker = "\033[31mERR \033[0m"
426	        else:
427	            marker = "\033[90mSKIP\033[0m"
428	
429	        print(f"  {r.tab:<12} {r.viewport_name:<14} {size_str:>10} "
430	              f"{r.pane_width:>7.0f}px {r.content_width:>7.0f}px "
431	              f"{r.dead_space_px:>7.0f}px {r.dead_space_pct:>5.1f}% {marker:>8}")
432	
433	    print("-" * 80)
434	    total = len(results)
435	    print(f"  TOTAL: {total}  |  PASS: {pass_count}  |  WARN: {warn_count}  "
436	          f"|  FAIL: {fail_count}")
437	
438	    if fail_count == 0 and warn_count == 0:
439	        print("\033[32m  ALL COMBOS PASS\033[0m")
440	    elif fail_count == 0:
441	        print(f"\033[33m  {warn_count} warnings, 0 failures\033[0m")
442	    else:
443	        print(f"\033[31m  {fail_count} FAILURES detected\033[0m")
444	    print("=" * 80 + "\n")
445	
446	    return 1 if fail_count > 0 else 0
447	
448	
449	def _write_report(results: list[ComboResult]) -> Path:
450	    """Write JSON report to disk."""
451	    report = {
452	        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
453	        "thresholds": {
454	            "pass_pct": PASS_THRESHOLD_PCT,
455	            "warn_pct": WARN_THRESHOLD_PCT,
456	        },
457	        "summary": {
458	            "total": len(results),
459	            "pass": sum(1 for r in results if r.verdict == "PASS"),
460	            "warn": sum(1 for r in results if r.verdict == "WARN"),
461	            "fail": sum(1 for r in results if r.verdict == "FAIL"),
462	            "error": sum(1 for r in results if r.verdict == "ERROR"),
463	        },
464	        "results": [asdict(r) for r in results],
465	    }
466	    report_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "audit_report.json"
467	    report_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
468	    log.info("Report written to %s", report_path)
469	    return report_path
470	
471	
472	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
473	# Main
474	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
475	
476	async def async_main(args: argparse.Namespace) -> int:
477	    """Entry point for the adaptive audit."""
478	    OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
479	    SCREENSHOT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
480	
481	    # Filter tabs if specified
482	    tabs = TABS
483	    if args.tabs:
484	        requested = [t.strip() for t in args.tabs.split(",")]
485	        tabs = [t for t in requested if t in TABS]
486	        if not tabs:
487	            log.error("No valid tabs in --tabs=%s. Available: %s", args.tabs, TABS)
488	            return 1
489	
490	    # Filter viewports if specified
491	    viewports = VIEWPORTS
492	    if args.viewports:
493	        requested_vp = [v.strip() for v in args.viewports.split(",")]
494	        viewports = [v for v in VIEWPORTS if v["name"] in requested_vp]
495	        if not viewports:
496	            log.error("No valid viewports in --viewports=%s", args.viewports)
497	            return 1
498	
499	    port = args.port
500	    server_proc = None
501	
502	    # Start server if needed
503	    if not args.no_server:
504	        server_proc = _start_server(port)
505	        healthy = await _wait_for_server(port)
506	        if not healthy:
507	            log.error("Server failed to start on port %d within timeout", port)
508	            if server_proc:
509	                _stop_server(server_proc)
510	            return 1
511	
512	    try:
513	        results = await run_audit(port, tabs, viewports)
514	        _write_report(results)
515	        exit_code = _print_summary(results)
516	        return exit_code
517	    finally:
518	        if server_proc:
519	            _stop_server(server_proc)
520	
521	
522	def main() -> None:
523	    """CLI entry point."""
524	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
525	        description="POLARIS Adaptive Layout Audit — dead-space verification",
526	    )
527	    parser.add_argument(
528	        "--port", type=int, default=DEFAULT_PORT,
529	        help=f"Server port (default: {DEFAULT_PORT})",
530	    )
531	    parser.add_argument(
532	        "--no-server", action="store_true",
533	        help="Skip auto-starting the server (assumes already running)",
534	    )
535	    parser.add_argument(
536	        "--tabs", type=str, default="",
537	        help="Comma-separated tab filter (e.g., report,advanced)",
538	    )
539	    parser.add_argument(
540	        "--viewports", type=str, default="",
541	        help="Comma-separated viewport filter (e.g., fullhd,laptop)",
542	    )
543	    args = parser.parse_args()
544	    exit_code = asyncio.run(async_main(args))
545	    sys.exit(exit_code)
546	
547	
548	if __name__ == "__main__":
549	    main()
