1	"""
2	Playwright automated audit of POLARIS pipeline HTML dashboard.
3	
4	Headless browser audit that validates all 10 dashboard sections
5	are populated, reasoning content is present, and no placeholder
6	text remains.
7	
8	15 checks:
9	  1. Dashboard loads
10	  2. All 10 sections exist
11	  3. Pipeline overview has duration
12	  4. Planning reasoning non-empty
13	  5. Search results table populated
14	  6. Fetch table populated
15	  7. STORM transcripts present
16	  8. STORM Q&A content
17	  9. Evidence funnel numbers
18	  10. Verification reasoning present
19	  11. Iteration decisions present
20	  12. Synthesis reasoning present
21	  13. Quality gates table
22	  14. LLM call log complete
23	  15. No placeholder text
24	
25	Output:
26	  - Screenshots: outputs/dashboard_audit/  (full page + per-section)
27	  - JSON report: outputs/dashboard_audit/audit_report.json
28	  - Exit code: 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
29	
30	CLI: python scripts/playwright_audit.py \
31	       --html outputs/dashboard_V001.html \
32	       --output outputs/dashboard_audit/
33	"""
34	
35	import argparse
36	import json
37	import os
38	import sys
39	import time
40	from pathlib import Path
41	
42	
43	def _check_playwright_installed() -> bool:
44	    """Check if playwright and chromium are available."""
45	    try:
46	        from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
47	        return True
48	    except ImportError:
49	        return False
50	
51	
52	def run_audit(html_path: str, output_dir: str) -> dict:
53	    """Run all 15 audit checks against the HTML dashboard.
54	
55	    Args:
56	        html_path: Path to the HTML dashboard file.
57	        output_dir: Directory for screenshots and report.
58	
59	    Returns:
60	        Audit report dict with pass/fail per check.
61	    """
62	    from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright
63	
64	    output = Path(output_dir)
65	    output.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
66	    screenshots_dir = output / "screenshots"
67	    screenshots_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
68	
69	    # Convert to file:// URL
70	    abs_path = Path(html_path).resolve()
71	    if not abs_path.exists():
72	        print(f"ERROR: HTML file not found: {abs_path}")
73	        sys.exit(1)
74	
75	    file_url = abs_path.as_uri()
76	    print(f"Auditing: {file_url}")
77	
78	    results = []
79	
80	    def _check(check_num: int, name: str, passed: bool, detail: str = "") -> None:
81	        status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
82	        icon = "[+]" if passed else "[-]"
83	        results.append({
84	            "check": check_num,
85	            "name": name,
86	            "passed": passed,
87	            "detail": detail,
88	        })
89	        print(f"  {icon} Check {check_num:2d}: {name} — {status}"
90	              + (f" ({detail})" if detail else ""))
91	
92	    with sync_playwright() as pw:
93	        browser = pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
94	        context = browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1400, "height": 900})
95	        page = context.new_page()
96	
97	        # Navigate to dashboard
98	        page.goto(file_url, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
99	        page.wait_for_timeout(500)
100	
101	        # -- Check 1: Dashboard loads --
102	        title = page.title()
103	        _check(1, "Dashboard loads", bool(title), f"title='{title}'")
104	
105	        # -- Check 2: All 10 sections exist --
106	        sections = page.query_selector_all("section[id]")
107	        section_count = len(sections)
108	        _check(2, "All 10 sections exist", section_count >= 10,
109	               f"found {section_count} sections")
110	
111	        # Take full-page screenshot
112	        page.screenshot(path=str(screenshots_dir / "full_page.png"), full_page=True)
113	
114	        # Expand all details for content checks
115	        page.evaluate("""
116	            document.querySelectorAll('details').forEach(d => d.open = true);
117	        """)
118	        page.wait_for_timeout(300)
119	
120	        # -- Check 3: Pipeline overview has duration --
121	        overview = page.query_selector("#overview")
122	        overview_text = overview.inner_text() if overview else ""
123	        has_duration = any(
124	            unit in overview_text for unit in ["ms", "min", "s"]
125	        )
126	        _check(3, "Pipeline overview has duration", has_duration,
127	               f"overview text length={len(overview_text)}")
128	
129	        # -- Check 4: Planning reasoning non-empty --
130	        planning = page.query_selector("#planning")
131	        planning_pres = planning.query_selector_all("pre") if planning else []
132	        plan_reasoning_found = False
133	        for pre in planning_pres:
134	            text = pre.inner_text()
135	            if len(text) > 100:
136	                plan_reasoning_found = True
137	                break
138	        _check(4, "Planning reasoning non-empty", plan_reasoning_found,
139	               f"{len(planning_pres)} pre blocks")
140	
141	        # -- Check 5: Search results table populated --
142	        search_table = page.query_selector("#search")
143	        search_rows = 0
144	        if search_table:
145	            search_rows = len(search_table.query_selector_all("tbody tr"))
146	        _check(5, "Search results table populated", search_rows > 1,
147	               f"{search_rows} rows")
148	
149	        # -- Check 6: Fetch table populated --
150	        fetch_table = page.query_selector("#fetch")
151	        fetch_rows = 0
152	        if fetch_table:
153	            fetch_rows = len(fetch_table.query_selector_all("tbody tr"))
154	        _check(6, "Fetch table populated", fetch_rows > 1,
155	               f"{fetch_rows} rows")
156	
157	        # -- Check 7: STORM transcripts present --
158	        storm_rounds = page.query_selector_all(".storm-round")
159	        _check(7, "STORM transcripts present", len(storm_rounds) > 0,
160	               f"{len(storm_rounds)} rounds")
161	
162	        # -- Check 8: STORM Q&A content --
163	        storm_qa_valid = True
164	        storm_qa_detail = ""
165	        if storm_rounds:
166	            for i, round_el in enumerate(storm_rounds[:5]):
167	                q_el = round_el.query_selector(".storm-q")
168	                a_el = round_el.query_selector(".storm-a")
169	                q_text = q_el.inner_text() if q_el else ""
170	                a_text = a_el.inner_text() if a_el else ""
171	                if not q_text.strip() or not a_text.strip():
172	                    storm_qa_valid = False
173	                    storm_qa_detail = f"round {i}: q={len(q_text)} a={len(a_text)}"
174	                    break
175	        else:
176	            storm_qa_valid = False
177	            storm_qa_detail = "no rounds"
178	        _check(8, "STORM Q&A content", storm_qa_valid, storm_qa_detail)
179	
180	        # -- Check 9: Evidence funnel numbers --
181	        evidence_section = page.query_selector("#evidence")
182	        funnel_fills = (
183	            evidence_section.query_selector_all(".funnel-fill")
184	            if evidence_section else []
185	        )
186	        funnel_valid = False
187	        funnel_detail = f"{len(funnel_fills)} bars"
188	        for fill in funnel_fills:
189	            text = fill.inner_text().strip().replace(",", "")
190	            if text.isdigit() and int(text) > 0:
191	                funnel_valid = True
192	                break
193	        _check(9, "Evidence funnel numbers", funnel_valid, funnel_detail)
194	
195	        # -- Check 10: Verification reasoning present --
196	        verify_section = page.query_selector("#verification")
197	        verify_pres = (
198	            verify_section.query_selector_all("pre") if verify_section else []
199	        )
200	        verify_found = False
201	        for pre in verify_pres:
202	            if len(pre.inner_text()) > 50:
203	                verify_found = True
204	                break
205	        _check(10, "Verification reasoning present", verify_found,
206	               f"{len(verify_pres)} pre blocks")
207	
208	        # -- Check 11: Iteration decisions present --
209	        iter_cards = page.query_selector_all(".iteration-card")
210	        _check(11, "Iteration decisions present", len(iter_cards) > 0,
211	               f"{len(iter_cards)} cards")
212	
213	        # -- Check 12: Synthesis reasoning present --
214	        synth_section = page.query_selector("#synthesis")
215	        synth_cards = (
216	            synth_section.query_selector_all(".card") if synth_section else []
217	        )
218	        _check(12, "Synthesis reasoning present", len(synth_cards) > 0,
219	               f"{len(synth_cards)} cards")
220	
221	        # -- Check 13: Quality gates table --
222	        qg_section = page.query_selector("#quality-gates")
223	        qg_rows = 0
224	        if qg_section:
225	            qg_table = qg_section.query_selector("table")
226	            if qg_table:
227	                qg_rows = len(qg_table.query_selector_all("tbody tr"))
228	        _check(13, "Quality gates table", qg_rows >= 1,
229	               f"{qg_rows} rows")
230	
231	        # -- Check 14: LLM call log complete --
232	        llm_section = page.query_selector("#llm-calls")
233	        llm_rows = 0
234	        if llm_section:
235	            llm_table = llm_section.query_selector("table")
236	            if llm_table:
237	                llm_rows = len(llm_table.query_selector_all("tbody tr"))
238	        _check(14, "LLM call log complete", llm_rows >= 10,
239	               f"{llm_rows} rows")
240	
241	        # -- Check 15: No placeholder text --
242	        body_text = page.inner_text("body").lower()
243	        forbidden = ["todo", "placeholder", "coming soon"]
244	        found_placeholders = [
245	            word for word in forbidden if word in body_text
246	        ]
247	        _check(15, "No placeholder text", len(found_placeholders) == 0,
248	               f"found: {found_placeholders}" if found_placeholders else "clean")
249	
250	        # Take per-section screenshots
251	        section_ids = [
252	            "overview", "planning", "search-fetch", "storm", "evidence",
253	            "verification", "iterations", "synthesis", "quality-gates", "llm-calls",
254	        ]
255	        for sid in section_ids:
256	            section_el = page.query_selector(f"#{sid}")
257	            if section_el:
258	                try:
259	                    section_el.screenshot(
260	                        path=str(screenshots_dir / f"section_{sid}.png"),
261	                    )
262	                except Exception:
263	                    pass  # Section may be too small or hidden
264	
265	        # Take expanded full-page screenshot
266	        page.screenshot(
267	            path=str(screenshots_dir / "full_page_expanded.png"),
268	            full_page=True,
269	        )
270	
271	        browser.close()
272	
273	    # Build report
274	    passed = sum(1 for r in results if r["passed"])
275	    total = len(results)
276	    report = {
277	        "html_path": str(abs_path),
278	        "timestamp": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()),
279	        "summary": {
280	            "passed": passed,
281	            "failed": total - passed,
282	            "total": total,
283	            "all_passed": passed == total,
284	        },
285	        "checks": results,
286	    }
287	
288	    report_path = output / "audit_report.json"
289	    report_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
290	    print(f"\n  Report: {report_path}")
291	    print(f"  Screenshots: {screenshots_dir}")
292	    print(f"  Result: {passed}/{total} passed"
293	          + (" — ALL PASS" if passed == total else " — FAILURES DETECTED"))
294	
295	    return report

297	
298	def main() -> None:
299	    """CLI entry point."""
300	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
301	        description="Automated Playwright audit of POLARIS HTML dashboard.",
302	    )
303	    parser.add_argument(
304	        "--html",
305	        required=True,
306	        help="Path to the HTML dashboard file",
307	    )
308	    parser.add_argument(
309	        "--output",
310	        default="outputs/dashboard_audit",
311	        help="Output directory for screenshots and report (default: outputs/dashboard_audit)",
312	    )
313	    args = parser.parse_args()
314	
315	    if not _check_playwright_installed():
316	        print("ERROR: playwright not installed.")
317	        print("  Install with: pip install playwright && python -m playwright install chromium")
318	        sys.exit(1)
319	
320	    report = run_audit(args.html, args.output)
321	    sys.exit(0 if report["summary"]["all_passed"] else 1)

325	    main()

1	"""
2	Playwright-based visual audit for citation card redesign and popover resize.
3	
4	Validates CSS properties, DOM structure, hover interactions, and popover
5	geometry against the specification. Outputs a JSON report and console
6	summary with PASS/FAIL per check.
7	
8	Usage:
9	    python scripts/playwright_cite_card_audit.py [--port PORT] [--output-dir DIR]
10	
11	Requirements:
12	    - playwright (async_api)
13	    - scripts/live_server.py (FastAPI server)
14	    - scripts/static/js/test_report.js (test bibliography data)
15	"""
16

61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	# Utility: find a free TCP port
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	def find_free_port() -> int:
65	    """Bind to port 0 and let the OS assign a free port."""
66	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as sock:
67	        sock.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
68	        return sock.getsockname()[1]
69	
70	
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	# Utility: wait for server readiness
73	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
74	async def wait_for_server(port: int, timeout_s: int) -> bool:
75	    """Poll the server TCP port until it accepts connections or timeout."""
76	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout_s
77	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
78	        try:
79	            reader, writer = await asyncio.open_connection("127.0.0.1", port)
80	            writer.close()
81	            await writer.wait_closed()
82	            return True
83	        except (ConnectionRefusedError, OSError):
84	            await asyncio.sleep(SERVER_POLL_INTERVAL_S)
85	    return False

88	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
89	# Server lifecycle
90	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
91	def start_server(port: int, log_dir: Path) -> subprocess.Popen:
92	    """
93	    Start live_server.py on the given port with --no-tunnel.
94	
95	    CRITICAL: stdout/stderr redirected to a log file to avoid
96	    subprocess.PIPE deadlocks on Windows (MEMORY lesson #16).
97	    """
98	    log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
99	    server_log_path = log_dir / "cite_card_audit_server.log"
100	    server_log_handle = open(server_log_path, "w", encoding="utf-8")
101	
102	    cmd = [
103	        sys.executable,
104	        str(_LIVE_SERVER_SCRIPT),
105	        "--port", str(port),
106	        "--no-tunnel",
107	    ]
108	    logger.info("Starting server: %s", " ".join(cmd))
109	    logger.info("Server log: %s", server_log_path)
110	
111	    proc = subprocess.Popen(
112	        cmd,
113	        stdout=server_log_handle,
114	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
115	        cwd=str(_PROJECT_ROOT),
116	    )
117	    # Keep handle reference so it stays open while server runs
118	    proc._log_handle = server_log_handle  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
119	    return proc
120	
121	
122	def stop_server(proc: subprocess.Popen) -> None:
123	    """Terminate the server process and close the log handle."""
124	    if proc.poll() is None:
125	        logger.info("Terminating server (pid=%d)", proc.pid)
126	        proc.terminate()
127	        try:
128	            proc.wait(timeout=5)
129	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
130	            proc.kill()
131	            proc.wait(timeout=3)
132	    log_handle = getattr(proc, "_log_handle", None)
133	    if log_handle and not log_handle.closed:
134	        log_handle.close()

137	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
138	# Check result helpers
139	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
140	class CheckResult:
141	    """Container for a single audit check result."""
142	
143	    def __init__(self, check_id: str, description: str):
144	        self.check_id = check_id
145	        self.description = description
146	        self.passed: bool = False
147	        self.actual: Any = None
148	        self.expected: Any = None
149	        self.error: str | None = None
150	
151	    def set_pass(self, actual: Any = None, expected: Any = None) -> "CheckResult":
152	        self.passed = True
153	        self.actual = actual
154	        self.expected = expected
155	        return self
156	
157	    def set_fail(
158	        self, actual: Any = None, expected: Any = None, error: str | None = None
159	    ) -> "CheckResult":
160	        self.passed = False
161	        self.actual = actual
162	        self.expected = expected
163	        self.error = error
164	        return self
165	
166	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
167	        result = {
168	            "check_id": self.check_id,
169	            "description": self.description,
170	            "passed": self.passed,
171	            "actual": self.actual,
172	            "expected": self.expected,
173	        }
174	        if self.error:
175	            result["error"] = self.error
176	        return result

179	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
180	# Computed style helper (runs in browser context)
181	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
182	async def get_computed_style(page: Page, selector: str, prop: str) -> str:
183	    """Return a computed CSS property value for the first matching element."""
184	    return await page.evaluate(
185	        """
186	        ([sel, prop]) => {
187	            const el = document.querySelector(sel);
188	            if (!el) return '__NOT_FOUND__';
189	            return window.getComputedStyle(el)[prop];
190	        }
191	        """,
192	        [selector, prop],
193	    )
194	
195	
196	async def get_computed_style_nth(
197	    page: Page, selector: str, prop: str, index: int = 0
198	) -> str:
199	    """Return a computed CSS property for the Nth matching element."""
200	    return await page.evaluate(
201	        """
202	        ([sel, prop, idx]) => {
203	            const els = document.querySelectorAll(sel);
204	            if (!els[idx]) return '__NOT_FOUND__';
205	            return window.getComputedStyle(els[idx])[prop];
206	        }
207	        """,
208	        [selector, prop, index],
209	    )
210	
211	
212	async def element_count(page: Page, selector: str) -> int:
213	    """Return count of elements matching selector."""
214	    return await page.evaluate(
215	        "(sel) => document.querySelectorAll(sel).length", selector
216	    )
217	
218	
219	async def element_text(page: Page, selector: str) -> str:
220	    """Return textContent of first matching element."""
221	    return await page.evaluate(
222	        """
223	        (sel) => {
224	            const el = document.querySelector(sel);
225	            return el ? el.textContent.trim() : '__NOT_FOUND__';
226	        }
227	        """,
228	        selector,
229	    )

235	async def run_card_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
236	    """Execute all citation card visual checks."""
237	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
238	
239	    # C1: 5 .ws-cite-card elements exist

248	    # C2: .ws-cite-card-num has pill shape (border-radius >= 10px, background not empty)

251	    background = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-cite-card-num", "backgroundColor")

373	
374	    # C9: Card 1 (verified) badge has class 'verified' and green background
375	    c9 = CheckResult("C9", "Card 1 badge is 'verified' with green background")
376	    badge_1_classes = await page.evaluate(
377	        """
378	        () => {
379	            const card = document.querySelector('.ws-cite-card[data-cite-num="1"]');

382	            if (!badge) return '__NO_BADGE__';
383	            return badge.className;
384	        }
385	        """
386	    )
387	    badge_1_bg = await page.evaluate(
388	        """
389	        () => {
390	            const card = document.querySelector('.ws-cite-card[data-cite-num="1"]');
391	            if (!card) return '__NO_CARD__';
392	            const badge = card.querySelector('.ws-cite-card-badge');

403	            expected="class 'verified', green background",
404	        )
405	    else:
406	        c9.set_fail(
407	            actual={"classes": badge_1_classes, "background": badge_1_bg},

456	# Popover checks (P1 -- P6)
457	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
458	async def run_popover_checks(page: Page) -> list[CheckResult]:
459	    """Execute all popover visual checks."""
460	    results: list[CheckResult] = []
461	
462	    # P1: Hover card 1 for 300ms, popover appears
463	    p1 = CheckResult("P1", "Popover .ws-cite-popover appears on hover (300ms)")
464	    card_1 = page.locator('.ws-cite-card[data-cite-num="1"]')
465	    await card_1.hover()
466	    await page.wait_for_timeout(POPOVER_HOVER_MS)
467	    popover_visible = await page.evaluate(
468	        """
469	        () => {
470	            const pop = document.querySelector('.ws-cite-popover');
471	            if (!pop) return false;
472	            const rect = pop.getBoundingClientRect();
473	            return rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0;
474	        }
475	        """
476	    )
477	    if popover_visible:
478	        p1.set_pass(actual="visible", expected="visible")
479	    else:
480	        p1.set_fail(actual="not visible", expected="visible", error="Popover not found or zero-size")
481	    results.append(p1)
482	
483	    # P2: Popover width ~480px (470-490)
484	    p2 = CheckResult("P2", "Popover width is ~480px (470-490)")
485	    popover_width = await page.evaluate(
486	        """
487	        () => {
488	            const pop = document.querySelector('.ws-cite-popover');
489	            if (!pop) return -1;
490	            return pop.getBoundingClientRect().width;
491	        }
492	        """
493	    )
494	    if 470 <= popover_width <= 490:
495	        p2.set_pass(actual=popover_width, expected="470-490")
496	    else:
497	        p2.set_fail(
498	            actual=popover_width, expected="470-490",
499	            error=f"Width is {popover_width}px",
500	        )
501	    results.append(p2)
502	
503	    # P3: Popover max-height >= 600px
504	    p3 = CheckResult("P3", "Popover max-height >= 600px")
505	    popover_max_h = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-cite-popover", "maxHeight")
506	    max_h_px = _parse_px(popover_max_h)
507	    if max_h_px >= 600:
508	        p3.set_pass(actual=popover_max_h, expected=">= 600px")
509	    else:
510	        p3.set_fail(
511	            actual=popover_max_h, expected=">= 600px",
512	            error=f"max-height is {popover_max_h}",
513	        )
514	    results.append(p3)
515	
516	    # P4: .ws-popover-iframe-wrap height ~340px (330-350)
517	    p4 = CheckResult("P4", ".ws-popover-iframe-wrap height ~340px (330-350)")
518	    iframe_wrap_h = await page.evaluate(
519	        """
520	        () => {
521	            const wrap = document.querySelector('.ws-popover-iframe-wrap');
522	            if (!wrap) return -1;
523	            return wrap.getBoundingClientRect().height;
524	        }
525	        """
526	    )
527	    if 330 <= iframe_wrap_h <= 350:
528	        p4.set_pass(actual=iframe_wrap_h, expected="330-350")
529	    else:
530	        p4.set_fail(
531	            actual=iframe_wrap_h, expected="330-350",
532	            error=f"Height is {iframe_wrap_h}px",
533	        )
534	    results.append(p4)
535	
536	    # P5: .ws-popover-chrome padding ~10px 14px
537	    p5 = CheckResult("P5", ".ws-popover-chrome padding is approximately 10px 14px")
538	    chrome_pt = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-popover-chrome", "paddingTop")
539	    chrome_pr = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-popover-chrome", "paddingRight")
540	    chrome_pb = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-popover-chrome", "paddingBottom")
541	    chrome_pl = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-popover-chrome", "paddingLeft")
542	    pt = _parse_px(chrome_pt)
543	    pr = _parse_px(chrome_pr)
544	    pb = _parse_px(chrome_pb)
545	    pl = _parse_px(chrome_pl)
546	    # Allow +/- 2px tolerance
547	    vertical_ok = abs(pt - 10) <= 2 and abs(pb - 10) <= 2
548	    horizontal_ok = abs(pr - 14) <= 2 and abs(pl - 14) <= 2
549	    if vertical_ok and horizontal_ok:
550	        p5.set_pass(
551	            actual=f"{chrome_pt} {chrome_pr} {chrome_pb} {chrome_pl}",
552	            expected="~10px 14px 10px 14px",
553	        )
554	    else:
555	        p5.set_fail(
556	            actual=f"{chrome_pt} {chrome_pr} {chrome_pb} {chrome_pl}",
557	            expected="~10px 14px 10px 14px",
558	            error=f"Padding: {pt} {pr} {pb} {pl}",
559	        )
560	    results.append(p5)
561	
562	    # P6: .ws-popover-title-sm max-width >= 250px
563	    p6 = CheckResult("P6", ".ws-popover-title-sm max-width >= 250px")
564	    title_mw = await get_computed_style(page, ".ws-popover-title-sm", "maxWidth")
565	    mw_px = _parse_px(title_mw)
566	    if mw_px >= 250:
567	        p6.set_pass(actual=title_mw, expected=">= 250px")
568	    else:
569	        p6.set_fail(
570	            actual=title_mw, expected=">= 250px",
571	            error=f"max-width is {title_mw}",
572	        )
573	    results.append(p6)
574	
575	    # Move mouse away to dismiss popover
576	    await page.mouse.move(0, 0)
577	    await page.wait_for_timeout(200)
578	
579	    return results

619	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
620	# Pixel parsing helpers
621	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
622	def _parse_px(value: str) -> float:
623	    """Extract numeric pixel value from a CSS string like '10px' or '10.5px'.
624	
625	    Returns -1 if the value cannot be parsed.
626	    """
627	    if not value or value == "__NOT_FOUND__":
628	        return -1
629	    cleaned = value.strip().lower().replace("px", "")
630	    try:
631	        return float(cleaned)
632	    except ValueError:
633	        return -1
634	
635	
636	def _is_amber_background(bg: str) -> bool:
637	    """Check if an rgba/rgb color string is amber-ish (high R, mid G, low B)."""
638	    rgba = _parse_rgba(bg)
639	    if rgba is None:
640	        return False
641	    r, g, b, _a = rgba
642	    # Amber: R > G > B with G reasonably high
643	    # For rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.1) the computed value stays as-is in Chromium
644	    if r == 0 and g == 0 and b == 0:
645	        return False
646	    return r > g > b and g > 5
647	
648	
649	def _is_green_background(bg: str) -> bool:
650	    """Check if an rgba/rgb color string is green-ish (high G, lower R/B)."""
651	    rgba = _parse_rgba(bg)
652	    if rgba is None:
653	        return False
654	    r, g, b, _a = rgba
655	    # For rgba(74, 222, 128, 0.1) the computed value has G dominant
656	    if r == 0 and g == 0 and b == 0:
657	        return False
658	    return g > r and g > b
659	
660	
661	def _parse_rgba(color_str: str) -> tuple[float, float, float, float] | None:
662	    """Parse 'rgba(r, g, b, a)' or 'rgb(r, g, b)' into a tuple."""
663	    if not color_str:
664	        return None
665	    color_str = color_str.strip()
666	    if color_str.startswith("rgba(") and color_str.endswith(")"):
667	        inner = color_str[5:-1]
668	        parts = [p.strip() for p in inner.split(",")]
669	        if len(parts) == 4:
670	            try:
671	                return (float(parts[0]), float(parts[1]), float(parts[2]), float(parts[3]))
672	            except ValueError:
673	                return None
674	    elif color_str.startswith("rgb(") and color_str.endswith(")"):
675	        inner = color_str[4:-1]
676	        parts = [p.strip() for p in inner.split(",")]
677	        if len(parts) == 3:
678	            try:
679	                return (float(parts[0]), float(parts[1]), float(parts[2]), 1.0)
680	            except ValueError:
681	                return None
682	    return None

685	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
686	# Report generation
687	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
688	def generate_report(
689	    card_results: list[CheckResult],
690	    popover_results: list[CheckResult],
691	    screenshots: list[str],
692	    output_dir: Path,
693	) -> dict:
694	    """Build and write the JSON audit report."""
695	    all_results = card_results + popover_results
696	    total = len(all_results)
697	    passed = sum(1 for r in all_results if r.passed)
698	    failed = total - passed
699	
700	    report = {
701	        "audit": "cite_card_popover_audit",
702	        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
703	        "summary": {
704	            "total_checks": total,
705	            "passed": passed,
706	            "failed": failed,
707	            "pass_rate": f"{(passed / total * 100) if total > 0 else 0:.1f}%",
708	        },
709	        "card_checks": [r.to_dict() for r in card_results],
710	        "popover_checks": [r.to_dict() for r in popover_results],
711	        "screenshots": screenshots,
712	    }
713	
714	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
715	    report_path = output_dir / "cite_card_audit_report.json"
716	    with open(report_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
717	        json.dump(report, fh, indent=2, default=str)
718	    logger.info("Report written: %s", report_path)
719	
720	    return report
721	
722	
723	def print_console_summary(report: dict) -> None:
724	    """Print a human-readable PASS/FAIL summary to stdout."""
725	    summary = report["summary"]
726	    print("\n" + "=" * 60)
727	    print("  CITATION CARD AUDIT RESULTS")
728	    print("=" * 60)
729	    print(f"  Total checks: {summary['total_checks']}")
730	    print(f"  Passed:       {summary['passed']}")
731	    print(f"  Failed:       {summary['failed']}")
732	    print(f"  Pass rate:    {summary['pass_rate']}")
733	    print("-" * 60)
734	
735	    for section_name, section_key in [
736	        ("Card Checks", "card_checks"),
737	        ("Popover Checks", "popover_checks"),
738	    ]:
739	        print(f"\n  {section_name}:")
740	        for check in report[section_key]:
741	            marker = "[PASS]" if check["passed"] else "[FAIL]"
742	            line = f"    {marker} {check['check_id']}: {check['description']}"
743	            if not check["passed"] and check.get("error"):
744	                line += f"  -- {check['error']}"
745	            print(line)
746	
747	    print("\n  Screenshots:")
748	    for path in report.get("screenshots", []):
749	        print(f"    - {path}")
750	
751	    print("=" * 60 + "\n")
752

754	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
755	# Main audit workflow
756	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
757	async def run_audit(port: int, output_dir: Path) -> dict:
758	    """Execute the full cite card + popover audit."""
759	    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
760	
761	    # Read test_report.js content for injection
762	    if not _TEST_REPORT_JS.exists():
763	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Test data not found: {_TEST_REPORT_JS}")
764	    test_js_content = _TEST_REPORT_JS.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
765	
766	    async with async_playwright() as pw:
767	        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
768	        context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1440, "height": 900})
769	        page = await context.new_page()
770	
771	        # Set generous navigation timeout (dashboard loads heavy JS + SSE)
772	        page.set_default_navigation_timeout(PAGE_LOAD_TIMEOUT_MS)
773	
774	        # Navigate to the dashboard.
775	        # CRITICAL: Use "domcontentloaded" instead of "networkidle" because
776	        # the dashboard opens SSE streams that keep the network perpetually
777	        # active, causing "networkidle" to never fire.
778	        dashboard_url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}/"
779	        logger.info("Navigating to %s", dashboard_url)
780	        await page.goto(dashboard_url, wait_until="domcontentloaded")
781	
782	        # Wait for all CSS and JS to load (stylesheets, workspace_manager.js)
783	        logger.info("Waiting for page scripts to initialize...")
784	        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
785	
786	        # Verify key functions exist before injecting test data
787	        functions_ready = await page.evaluate(
788	            """
789	            () => {
790	                return typeof setWorkspacePhase === 'function'
791	                    && typeof renderCitationSidebar === 'function';
792	            }
793	            """
794	        )
795	        if not functions_ready:
796	            raise RuntimeError(
797	                "Dashboard JS not fully loaded: setWorkspacePhase or "
798	                "renderCitationSidebar not found after 3s wait"
799	            )
800	        logger.info("Dashboard JS functions confirmed available.")
801	
802	        # Inject test data via page.evaluate()
803	        logger.info("Injecting test_report.js content")
804	        await page.evaluate(test_js_content)
805	
806	        # Wait for DOM to settle after rendering citation cards
807	        await page.wait_for_timeout(DOM_SETTLE_MS)
808	
809	        # Run checks
810	        logger.info("Running citation card checks (C1-C11)...")
811	        card_results = await run_card_checks(page)
812	
813	        logger.info("Running popover checks (P1-P6)...")
814	        popover_results = await run_popover_checks(page)
815	
816	        logger.info("Capturing screenshots...")
817	        screenshots = await capture_screenshots(page, output_dir)
818	
819	        await browser.close()
820	
821	    # Generate report
822	    report = generate_report(card_results, popover_results, screenshots, output_dir)
823	    print_console_summary(report)
824	
825	    return report

829	# CLI entry point
830	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
831	def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
832	    """Parse command-line arguments."""
833	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
834	        description="Playwright audit for citation card redesign and popover resize"
835	    )
836	    parser.add_argument(
837	        "--port",
838	        type=int,
839	        default=0,
840	        help="Server port (0 = auto-detect free port)",
841	    )
842	    parser.add_argument(
843	        "--output-dir",
844	        type=str,
845	        default=str(_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR),
846	        help=f"Output directory for report and screenshots (default: {_DEFAULT_OUTPUT_DIR})",
847	    )
848	    return parser.parse_args()

850	
851	async def async_main() -> None:
852	    """Async entry point: start server, run audit, cleanup."""
853	    args = parse_args()
854	    port = args.port if args.port > 0 else find_free_port()
855	    output_dir = Path(args.output_dir)
856	
857	    logger.info("Audit output directory: %s", output_dir)
858	    logger.info("Using port: %d", port)
859	
860	    # Start the server
861	    server_proc = start_server(port, output_dir)
862	    try:
863	        # Wait for server to be ready
864	        logger.info(
865	            "Waiting up to %ds for server on port %d...",
866	            SERVER_READY_TIMEOUT_S, port,
867	        )
868	        ready = await wait_for_server(port, SERVER_READY_TIMEOUT_S)
869	        if not ready:
870	            raise RuntimeError(
871	                f"Server did not become ready within {SERVER_READY_TIMEOUT_S}s on port {port}"
872	            )
873	        logger.info("Server is ready.")
874	
875	        # Run the audit
876	        report = await run_audit(port, output_dir)
877	
878	        # Exit with non-zero if any checks failed
879	        if report["summary"]["failed"] > 0:
880	            sys.exit(1)
881	
882	    finally:
883	        stop_server(server_proc)
884	        logger.info("Server stopped. Audit complete.")
885	
886	
887	def main() -> None:
888	    """Synchronous entry point."""
889	    asyncio.run(async_main())
890	
891	
892	if __name__ == "__main__":
893	    main()

4	Tests that citation cards survive collapse, scroll_sync while collapsed, re-expand,
5	and accordion toggling -- using a real appendReportBlock() call that triggers
6	IntersectionObserver-based scroll_sync (unlike test_report.js which only calls
7	renderCitationSidebar directly).

9	Usage:
10	    python scripts/playwright_cite_collapse_audit.py
11	
12	Outputs screenshots to outputs/cite_collapse_visual/

37	def _find_free_port() -> int:
38	    """Bind to port 0 and let the OS assign a free port."""
39	    with socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) as s:
40	        s.bind(("127.0.0.1", 0))
41	        return s.getsockname()[1]
42	
43	
44	def _wait_for_server(port: int, timeout: float = 15.0) -> bool:
45	    """Poll until the server accepts TCP connections."""
46	    deadline = time.monotonic() + timeout
47	    while time.monotonic() < deadline:
48	        try:
49	            with socket.create_connection(("127.0.0.1", port), timeout=1.0):
50	                return True
51	        except OSError:
52	            time.sleep(0.3)
53	    return False

141	async def run_audit():
142	    """Main audit function."""
143	    from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
144	
145	    SCREENSHOT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
146	
147	    # Find a free port
148	    port = _find_free_port()
149	    url = f"http://127.0.0.1:{port}"
150	    print(f"[AUDIT] Using port {port}, URL: {url}")
151	
152	    # Start the live server with stdout redirected to log file (Windows deadlock fix)
153	    SERVER_LOG.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
154	    log_fh = open(str(SERVER_LOG), "w", encoding="utf-8")
155	    server_proc = subprocess.Popen(
156	        [
157	            sys.executable, str(SERVER_SCRIPT),
158	            "--no-tunnel",
159	            "--port", str(port),
160	        ],
161	        stdout=log_fh,
162	        stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
163	        cwd=str(PROJECT_ROOT),
164	    )
165	    print(f"[AUDIT] Server PID={server_proc.pid}, log={SERVER_LOG}")
166	
167	    try:
168	        # Wait for server to be ready
169	        if not _wait_for_server(port, timeout=15.0):
170	            print("[AUDIT] FATAL: Server did not start within 15 seconds.")
171	            return
172	
173	        print("[AUDIT] Server is up. Launching Playwright...")
174	
175	        async with async_playwright() as pw:
176	            browser = await pw.chromium.launch(headless=True)
177	            context = await browser.new_context(viewport={"width": 1440, "height": 900})
178	            page = await context.new_page()
179	
180	            # Collect console errors for debugging
181	            console_errors = []
182	            page.on("console", lambda msg: console_errors.append(f"[{msg.type}] {msg.text}") if msg.type == "error" else None)
183	
184	            # Navigate with domcontentloaded (NOT networkidle -- SSE keeps conn open)
185	            print(f"[AUDIT] Navigating to {url} ...")
186	            await page.goto(url, wait_until="domcontentloaded", timeout=15000)
187	
188	            # Wait for JS init
189	            await page.wait_for_timeout(PAGE_LOAD_WAIT_MS)
190	            print("[AUDIT] Page loaded. Injecting state...")
191	
192	            # Inject realistic state with REAL report block
193	            result = await page.evaluate(JS_INJECT_STATE)
194	            print(f"[AUDIT] State injection result: {result}")
195	
196	            # Wait for scroll_sync to initialize (appendReportBlock has 100ms setTimeout)
197	            await page.wait_for_timeout(JS_INIT_WAIT_MS)
198	
199	            # ==================================================================
200	            # Results tracking
201	            # ==================================================================
202	            results = {}
203	            pass_count = 0
204	            fail_count = 0
205	            step_data = {}
206	
207	            def record(step_name, key, value, expected=None, assertion_fn=None):
208	                nonlocal pass_count, fail_count
209	                step_data.setdefault(step_name, {})
210	                step_data[step_name][key] = value
211	                if assertion_fn is not None:
212	                    passed = assertion_fn(value)
213	                    status = "PASS" if passed else "FAIL"
214	                    if passed:
215	                        pass_count += 1
216	                    else:
217	                        fail_count += 1
218	                    results[f"{step_name}.{key}"] = {
219	                        "value": value,
220	                        "expected": expected,
221	                        "status": status,
222	                    }
223	                    print(f"  [{status}] {step_name}.{key} = {value} (expected: {expected})")
224	                else:
225	                    results[f"{step_name}.{key}"] = {"value": value, "status": "INFO"}
226	                    print(f"  [INFO] {step_name}.{key} = {value}")
227	
228	            # ==================================================================
229	            # Step 0: Verify initial state
230	            # ==================================================================
231	            print("\n=== Step 0: Verify initial state ===")
232	            initial_count = await page.evaluate(JS_COUNT_CITE_CARDS)
233	            badge_text = await page.evaluate(JS_GET_BADGE_TEXT)
234	            cite_class = await page.evaluate(JS_CHECK_CITATIONS_CLASS)
235	            html_len = await page.evaluate(JS_GET_CITATION_LIST_HTML_LENGTH)
236	            html_snippet = await page.evaluate(JS_GET_CITATION_LIST_SNIPPET)
237	
238	            record("step0", "cite_card_count", initial_count, "> 0", lambda v: v > 0)

I've now read all 16 files in the shard. Let me compile findings and write the TSV.

Most of these files are cleanly named (test scripts, audit scripts, playwright audits). The notable flags:

1. `pipeline_diced_preflight.py` — "DICED" is a coined acronym (undefined anywhere in the file), and function names like `dice_d0_scope_no_source_drop`, `dice_fx06_population_coupling` etc. are cryptic internal jargon codes (D0-D9, FX06) that read as unprofessional/undocumented shorthand for external review. Also `_SCRATCH` referencing directories "deepfix_replay_forensic2" / "deepfix_run1_audit" — informal.
2. Test scripts named by ticket/test numbers (`pg_test_051`, `pg_test_060`, etc.) are standard test-numbering convention — acceptable, KEEP-NOTED not remedial renames needed generally, but I'll flag a couple of the worst ones.

Let me write the TSV.
